You can cast the Illusion version of each spell without expending a spell slot, but casting it without a slot halves the creature's Hit Points. Once you cast either spell without a spell slot, you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can cast the spell in that way again.
illusionist.features.2.name: Phantasmal Creatures
illusionist.features.3.description:
When a creature hits you with an attack roll, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to interpose an illusory duplicate of yourself between the attacker and yourself. The attack automatically misses you, then the illusion dissipates.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 2+ spell slot (no action required).
illusionist.features.3.name: Illusory Self
illusionist.features.4.description:
You have learned to weave shadow magic into your illusions to give them a semi-reality. When you cast an Illusion spell with a spell slot, you can choose one inanimate, nonmagical object that is part of the illusion and make that object real. You can do this on your turn as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} while the spell is ongoing. The object remains real for 1 minute, during which it can't deal damage or give any conditions.

For example, you can create an illusion of a bridge over a chasm and then make it real and cross it.
illusionist.features.4.name: Illusory Reality
illusionist.name: Иллюзионист
inquisition.description: The Inquisition Domain reflects the order of the multiverse and the rejection of tainted magic—so far as certain celestial powers see fit. Only the divine casters are pure and fit for use. Since arcane magic is strong enough to challenge the gods, divine beings of this domain, such as the Arch Seraph Empyreus, demand magic-using mortals be kept in check. Most zealots root out all arcanists, while some strike fragile truces when complete removal isn't feasible. Cleanse the Heretics.
inquisition.features.0.description: Your connection to this divine domain ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach a Cleric level specified in the Inquisition Domain Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
inquisition.features.0.name: Inquisition Domain Spells
inquisition.features.1.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Force damage to the target. If the creature is {@status Concentrating|XPHB} on a spell, you deal an extra {@damage 2d6} Force damage instead. At Cleric level 14, the extra Force damage increases to {@damage 2d6}, or {@damage 3d6} if the creature is {@status Concentrating|XPHB} on a spell. If a creature fails its saving throw to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB} as a result of taking damage from this feature, you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the extra Force damage dealt. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
inquisition.features.1.name: Witch Hunter's Strike
inquisition.features.2.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to bestow a temporary resilience against arcane harm for 10 minutes. Choose a creature you can see (including yourself) within 30 feet of yourself. The chosen creature gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 1d10} plus your Cleric level. While a creature has {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} granted by your Spell Shield, the creature has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells, and it has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to the damage of spells. If any of these {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} remain when Spell Shield ends, they vanish.
inquisition.features.2.name: Spell Shield
inquisition.features.3.description: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} in response to a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself using a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to cast a spell, you can force the creature to make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 1d6} Force damage, plus another {@damage 1d6} per level of the spell slot the creature expended. Cantrips are considered level 1 spells for this ability. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage instead. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
inquisition.features.3.name: Rebuke Invoker
inquisition.features.4.description: Spell Shield can target a number of creatures up to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of one creature).
inquisition.features.4.name: Supernal Safeguard
inquisition.name: Домен Инквизиции
inquisitive.description: As an Inquisitive, you excel at rooting out secrets and unraveling mysteries. You rely on your sharp eye for detail and your finely honed ability to read the words and deeds of other creatures to determine their true intent. You excel at defeating creatures that hide among and prey upon ordinary folk, and your mastery of lore and your keen deductions make you well equipped to expose and end hidden evils.
inquisitive.features.0.description: Whenever you make a Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check to determine whether a creature is lying to you, treat a roll of 7 or lower on the {@dice d20} as an 8.
inquisitive.features.0.name: Ear for Deceit
inquisitive.features.1.description: You can use a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to make a Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check to spot a hidden creature or object or to make an Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check to uncover or decipher clues.
inquisitive.features.1.name: Eye for Detail
inquisitive.features.2.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can make a Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check against a creature you can see that isn't {@condition Incapacitated}, contested by the target's Charisma ({@skill Deception}) check. If you succeed, you can use your Sneak Attack against that target even if you don't have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack roll, but not if you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on it. This benefit lasts for 1 minute or until you successfully use this feature against a different target.
inquisitive.features.2.name: Insightful Fighting
inquisitive.name: Сыщик
inquisitive.shortDescription: Root Out Secrets and Unravel Mysteries
kensei.description: Monks of the Kensei train relentlessly with their weapons, to the point where the weapon becomes an extension of the body. Founded on a mastery of sword fighting, the tradition has expanded to include many different weapons. A kensei sees a weapon in much the same way a calligrapher or painter regards a pen or brush. Whatever the weapon, the kensei views it as a tool used to express the beauty and precision of the martial arts. That such mastery makes a kensei a peerless warrior is but a side effect of intense devotion, practice, and study.
kensei.features.0.description: When you choose this tradition at 3rd level, your special martial arts training leads you to master the use of certain weapons. You gain the following benefits.
kensei.features.0.details.agileParry: {@b Agile Parry.} If you make an unarmed strike as part of the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn and are holding a kensei weapon, you can use it to defend yourself if it is a melee weapon. You gain a +2 bonus to AC until the start of your next turn, while the weapon is in your hand and you aren't {@condition incapacitated|XPHB}.
kensei.features.0.details.kenseiShot: {@b Kensei's Shot.} You can use a bonus action on your turn to make your ranged attacks with a kensei weapon more deadly. When you do so, any target you hit with a ranged attack using a kensei weapon takes an extra {@damage 1d4} damage of the weapon's type. You retain this benefit until the end of the current turn.
kensei.features.0.details.kenseiWeapons: {@b Kensei Weapons.} Choose two types of weapons to be your kensei weapons: one melee weapon and one ranged weapon. Each of these weapons can be any {@filter simple or martial weapon that lacks the heavy and special properties|XPHB}. The longbow is also a valid choice. You gain proficiency with these weapons if you don't already have it. Weapons of the chosen types are monk weapons for you. Many of this tradition's features work only with your kensei weapons. When you reach 6th, 11th, and 17th level in this class, you can choose another type of weapon—either melee or ranged—to be a kensei weapon for you, following the criteria above.
kensei.features.0.details.wayOfTheBrush: {@b Way of the Brush.} You gain proficiency with your choice of {@item calligrapher's supplies|XPHB} or {@item painter's supplies|XPHB}.
kensei.features.0.name: Path of the Kensei
kensei.features.1.description: At 6th level, you extend your ki into your kensei weapons, granting you the following benefits.
kensei.features.1.details.deftStrike: {@b Deft Strike.} When you hit a target with a kensei weapon, you can spend 1 ki point to cause the weapon to deal extra damage to the target equal to your Martial Arts die. You can use this feature only once on each of your turns.
kensei.features.1.details.magicKenseiWea: {@b Magic Kensei Weapons.} Your attacks with your kensei weapons count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.
kensei.features.1.name: One with the Blade
kensei.features.2.description: At 11th level, you gain the ability to augment your weapons further with your ki. As a bonus action, you can expend up to 3 ki points to grant one kensei weapon you touch a bonus to attack and damage rolls when you attack with it. The bonus equals the number of ki points you spent. This bonus lasts for 1 minute or until you use this feature again. This feature has no effect on a magic weapon that already has a bonus to attack and damage rolls.
kensei.features.2.name: Sharpen the Blade
kensei.features.3.description: At 17th level, your mastery of weapons grants you extraordinary accuracy. If you miss with an attack roll using a monk weapon on your turn, you can reroll it. You can use this feature only once on each of your turns.
kensei.features.3.name: Unerring Accuracy
kensei.name: Путь Кенсей
kensei.shortDescription: Master Weapons as Extensions of Self
knowledge.description: The Knowledge Domain values learning and understanding above all. Clerics who tap into this domain study esoteric lore, collect old tomes, delve into secret places, and examine the processes of the mind. To them, knowledge is more valuable than material wealth, and learning is an act of worship. Libraries, universities, and other educational institutions also draw on the power of the Knowledge Domain. Gods of knowledge vary from masters of arcane magic to patrons of craft and invention. In Faerûn, Clerics of the Knowledge Domain worship deities of learning and ingenuity such as {@deity Oghma} and {@deity Gond}, among many others. Unearth Secrets and Master the Mind.
knowledge.features.0.description: You gain proficiency with one type of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} of your choice and in two of the following skills of your choice: {@skill Arcana}, {@skill History}, {@skill Nature}, or {@skill Religion}. You have {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in those two skills.
knowledge.features.0.name: Blessings of Knowledge
knowledge.features.1.description: When you reach a Cleric level specified in the Knowledge Domain Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
knowledge.features.1.name: Knowledge Domain Spells
knowledge.features.2.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to manifest your magical knowledge. Choose one spell from the Divination school on the Knowledge Domain Spells table that you have prepared. As part of that action, you cast that spell without expending a spell slot or needing Material components.
knowledge.features.2.name: Mind Magic
knowledge.features.3.description: You gain {@sense telepathy|XPHB} out to 60 feet. When you use this telepathy, you can simultaneously contact a number of creatures equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of one). Additionally, you gain proficiency in Intelligence saving throws. If you already have this proficiency, you instead gain saving throw proficiency with one ability in which you lack it.
knowledge.features.3.name: Unfettered Mind
knowledge.features.4.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you magically expand your mind to the future. For 1 hour, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule D20 Tests|XPHB}. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of this feature by expending a level 6+ spell slot (no action required).
knowledge.features.4.name: Divine Foreknowledge
knowledge.name: Домен Знаний
knowledge.shortDescription: Unearth Secrets and Master the Mind
land.description: The Circle of the Land comprises mystics and sages who safeguard ancient knowledge and rites. These Druids meet within sacred circles of trees or standing stones to whisper primal secrets in Druidic. The circle's wisest members preside as the chief priests of their communities.
land.features.0.description: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, choose one type of land: arid, polar, temperate, or tropical. Consult the table below that corresponds to the chosen type; you have the spells listed for your Druid level and lower prepared.
land.features.0.name: Circle of the Land Spells
land.features.1.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape and choose a point within 60 feet of yourself. Vitality-giving flowers and life-draining thorns appear for a moment in a 10-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere|XPHB} centered on that point. Each creature of your choice in the {@variantrule Sphere|XPHB} must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC, taking {@damage 2d6} Necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. One creature of your choice in that area regains {@dice 2d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. The damage and healing increase by {@dice 1d6} when you reach Druid levels 10 ({@dice 3d6}) and 14 ({@dice 4d6}).
land.features.1.name: Land's Aid
land.features.2.description: You can cast one of the level 1+ spells that you have prepared from your Circle Spells feature without expending a spell slot, and you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you do so again. In addition, when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you can choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level that is equal to or less than half your Druid level (round up), and none of them can be level 6+. For example, if you're a level 6 Druid, you can recover up to three levels' worth of spell slots. You can recover a level 3 spell slot, a level 2 and a level 1 spell slot, or three level 1 spell slots. Once you recover spell slots with this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
land.features.2.name: Natural Recovery
land.features.3.description: You are immune to the {@condition Poisoned} condition, and you have Resistance to a damage type associated with your current land choice in the Circle Spells feature, as shown in the Nature's Ward table.
land.features.3.name: Nature's Ward
land.features.4.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape and cause spectral trees and vines to appear in a 15-foot {@variantrule Cube|XPHB} on the ground within 120 feet of yourself. They last there for 1 minute or until you have the {@condition Incapacitated} condition or die. You and your allies have {@variantrule Half Cover|XPHB} while in that area, and your allies gain the current Resistance of your Nature's Ward while there. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can move the {@variantrule Cube|XPHB} up to 60 feet to ground within 120 feet of yourself.
land.features.4.name: Nature's Sanctuary
land.name: Круг Земли
land.shortDescription: Celebrate Connection to the Natural World
leaden-crown.description: In contrast to the esoteric ideals of other orders, Warriors of the Leaden Crown have practical aims: the self-governance of all people. These Monks see history as a series of clashes between groups of powerful beings where Humanoids are at best collateral damage and at worst disposable pawns. To break this cycle of dependence and destruction, Warriors of the Leaden Crown master mental abilities to fight against the otherworldly powers of the multiverse and protect Humanoid sovereignty. One component of this plan involves training to do battle with powerful planar foes. The other equally important component is ensuring that Humanoid societies are prepared to overthrow those already dominating them. To that end, these Monks seek political positions that place them in or near decision-making roles where they can influence others to fight back against Arch Seraphs, Arch Daemons, and Primordials.
leaden-crown.features.0.description: Your martial arts are enhanced by a capacity for telekinetic strikes. During your turn, your reach is 5 feet greater with {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB}. In addition, when you hit a creature with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} as part of the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can choose to have it deal your choice of Psychic damage or its normal damage type.
leaden-crown.features.0.name: Subtle Hand
leaden-crown.features.1.description: Your psychic powers have manifested in the ability to cast certain spells. You know the {@spell Mage Hand|XPHB} cantrip. You can cast it without Verbal or Somatic components, and you can make the spectral hand {@condition Invisible|XPHB}. In addition, you can cast certain spells by expending Focus Points. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and expend 1 Focus Point to cast {@spell Detect Evil and Good|XPHB} or {@spell Protection from Evil and Good|XPHB}. You can also take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and expend 2 Focus Points to cast {@spell Hold Person|XPHB}, {@spell Levitate|XPHB}, or {@spell Shatter|XPHB}. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells, and you can cast them without Material components.
leaden-crown.features.1.name: Psionic Prowess
leaden-crown.features.2.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} or Monk weapon, you can force it to make a Strength saving throw against your Focus Point save DC. On a failed save, you can move the target up to 10 feet toward or away from you.
leaden-crown.features.2.name: Unsubtle Strike
leaden-crown.features.3.description: Each time you hit a creature with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, it gains a Pressure Point. A creature loses all Pressure Points if you cause a different creature to gain a Pressure Point or after 1 minute, whichever comes first. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend 1 Focus Point to telekinetically crush a creature with 1 or more of your Pressure Points. The creature loses all Pressure Points and must make a Strength saving throw against your Focus Point save DC. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 1d8} Force damage per Pressure Point, and it has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only.
leaden-crown.features.3.name: Psychic Crush
leaden-crown.features.4.description: After much training, you have mastered the psionic disciplines necessary to defend mortals from planar threats. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can spend 5 Focus Points to cast {@spell Dispel Evil and Good|XPHB}, {@spell Hold Monster|XPHB}, {@spell Telekinesis|XPHB}, or {@spell Wall of Force|XPHB}. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells, and you can cast them without Material components.
leaden-crown.features.4.name: Psionic Mastery
leaden-crown.name: Воин Свинцовой Короны
life.description: The Life Domain focuses on the vibrant positive energy—one of the fundamental forces of the multiverse—that sustains all life. The gods of life promote vitality and health through healing the sick and wounded, caring for those in need, and driving away the forces of undeath and death. Almost any non-evil deity can claim influence over this domain, particularly agricultural deities, sun gods, gods of healing or endurance, and gods of home and community.
life.features.0.description: Your connection to this divine domain ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach a Cleric level specified in the Life Domain Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
life.features.0.name: Life Domain Spells
life.features.1.description: When a spell you cast with a spell slot restores {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to a creature, that creature regains additional {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} on the turn you cast the spell. The additional {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal 2 plus the spell slot's level.
life.features.1.name: Disciple of Life
life.features.2.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you present your {@item Holy Symbol|XPHB} and expend a use of your Channel Divinity to evoke healing energy that can restore a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to five times your Cleric level. Choose {@status Bloodied|XPHB} creatures within 30 feet of yourself (which can include you), and divide those {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} among them. This feature can restore a creature to no more than half its {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB} maximum.
life.features.2.name: Preserve Life
life.features.3.description: The healing spells you cast on others heal you as well. Immediately after you cast a spell with a spell slot that restores {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to one creature other than you, you regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to 2 plus the spell slot's level.
life.features.3.name: Blessed Healer
life.features.4.description: When you would normally roll one or more dice to restore {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} with a spell or Channel Divinity, don't roll those dice for the healing; instead use the highest number possible for each die. For example, instead of restoring {@dice 2d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to a creature, you restore 12.
life.features.4.name: Supreme Healing
life.name: Домен Жизни
light.description: Gods of light—including {@deity Helm}, {@deity Lathander}, {@deity Pholtus}, {@deity Branchala}, the Silver Flame, {@deity Belenus}, {@deity Apollo}, and {@deity Re-Horakhty}—promote the ideals of rebirth and renewal, truth, vigilance, and beauty, often using the symbol of the sun. Some of these gods are portrayed as the sun itself or as a charioteer who guides the sun across the sky. Others are tireless sentinels whose eyes pierce every shadow and see through every deception. Some are deities of beauty and artistry, who teach that art is a vehicle for the soul's improvement.
light.features.0.description: Your connection to this divine domain ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach a Cleric level specified in the Light Domain Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
light.features.0.name: Light Domain Spells
light.features.1.description: When a creature that you can see within 30 feet of yourself makes an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the attack roll, causing light to flare before it hits or misses. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
light.features.1.name: Warding Flare
light.features.2.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend a use of your Channel Divinity and present your {@item Holy Symbol|XPHB} to emit sunlight in a 30-foot-radius {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} originating from yourself. Each creature of your choice in that area must make a Constitution saving throw, taking Radiant damage equal to {@dice 2d10} plus your Cleric level on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. In addition, {@variantrule Magical Darkness|XPHB} in that area is dispelled.
light.features.2.name: Radiance of the Dawn
light.features.3.description: You regain all expended uses of your Warding Flare when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. In addition, whenever you use Warding Flare, you can give the target of the triggering attack a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 2d6} plus your Wisdom modifier.
light.features.3.name: Improved Warding Flare
light.features.4.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you cause yourself to emit an aura of sunlight that lasts for 1 minute or until you dismiss it (no action required). You emit {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 60-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 30 feet. Your enemies in the {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws against your Radiance of the Dawn and any spell that deals Fire or Radiant damage. You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
light.features.4.name: Corona of Light
light.name: Домен Света
light.shortDescription: Bearer of Light and Fire Against Darkness
living-crucible.description: Fighters who become Living Crucibles have discovered an external source of power. While most Fighters train in martial traditions or study the art of war, Living Crucibles hone their craft of alchemy to endure compounds poisonous to others. In exchange for this rigorous physical and mental preparation, these Fighters are able to temporarily push their bodies past their natural limits. Under the influence of their alchemical compounds, these warriors can see in darkness, enhance their speed, inure themselves to magical attacks, and more.
living-crucible.features.0.description: You learn to create alchemical compounds toxic to others but empowering to you.
living-crucible.features.0.details.compo: {@b Compounds.} You learn three compounds of your choice from the "Compound Options" section below. You learn two additional compounds of your choice when you reach Fighter levels 7, 10, and 15. Each time you learn new compounds, you can also replace one compound you know with a different one.
living-crucible.features.0.details.consu: {@b Consuming.} As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can consume a compound. You can consume a number of compounds up to your Constitution modifier (minimum of one). Once you reach this limit, you cannot benefit from more compounds until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can benefit from multiple compounds at the same time, but consuming multiple vials of the same compound provides no additional effects. Only you can benefit from your compounds. Any other creature that consumes a compound must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus your Intelligence modifier plus {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}) or have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute.
living-crucible.features.0.details.creat: {@b Creating.} Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} while holding {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB}, you can use that tool to magically produce any number of compounds. The compound appears in a vial, and the vial vanishes when the compound is consumed or poured out. If any compound remains when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, the compound and its vial vanish.
living-crucible.features.0.name: Compound Creator
living-crucible.features.1.description: Your long nights of vigilance have left your senses attuned to signs of danger, granting you the following benefits. You gain {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB}, and you gain proficiency with it. In addition, your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} is doubled for ability checks with {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB}.
living-crucible.features.1.name: Student of Alchemy
living-crucible.features.2.description: The number of compounds you can safely consume increases to three plus your Constitution modifier (minimum one). At Fighter level 18, the number of compounds you can safely consume increases to five plus your Constitution modifier (minimum one).
living-crucible.features.2.name: Living Cauldron
living-crucible.features.3.description: When you use a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to drink a compound, you can drink a second compound.
living-crucible.features.3.name: Rapid Consumption
living-crucible.features.4.description: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Poison damage. Also, as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can end the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition on yourself. When you end the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition on yourself in this way, you can choose to gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Fighter level. You regain the ability to gain these {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} after completing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
living-crucible.features.4.name: Toxin Transmutation
living-crucible.features.5.description: When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can replace one compound you know with another one.
living-crucible.features.5.name: Living Catalyst
living-crucible.name: Живой Тигель
living-crucible.shortDescription: Enhance Your Martial Prowess with Alchemy
long-death.description: Monks of the Way of the Long Death are obsessed with the meaning and mechanics of dying. They capture creatures and prepare elaborate experiments to capture, record, and understand the moments of their demise. They use this knowledge to guide their understanding of martial arts, yielding a deadly fighting style.
long-death.features.0.description: Starting when you choose this tradition at 3rd level, your study of death allows you to extract vitality from another creature as it nears its demise. When you reduce a creature within 5 feet of you to 0 hit points, you gain temporary hit points equal to your Wisdom modifier + your monk level (minimum of 1 temporary hit point).
long-death.features.0.name: Прикосновение смерти
long-death.features.1.description: At 6th level, you gain the ability to unsettle or terrify those around you as an action, for your soul has been touched by the shadow of death. When you take this action, each creature within 30 feet of you that can see you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened|XPHB} of you until the end of your next turn.
long-death.features.1.name: Hour of Reaping
long-death.features.2.description: Beginning at 11th level, you use your familiarity with death to escape its grasp. When you are reduced to 0 hit points, you can expend 1 ki point (no action required) to have 1 hit point instead.
long-death.features.2.name: Mastery of Death
long-death.features.3.description: Starting at 17th level, your touch can channel the energy of death into a creature. As an action, you touch one creature within 5 feet of you, and you expend 1 to 10 ki points. The target must make a Constitution saving throw, and it takes {@damage 2d10} necrotic damage per ki point spent on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
long-death.features.3.name: Touch of the Long Death
long-death.name: Путь Долгой Смерти
lore.description: Bards of the College of Lore collect spells and secrets from diverse sources, such as scholarly tomes, mystical rites, and peasant tales. The college's members gather in libraries and universities to share their lore with one another. They also meet at festivals or affairs of state, where they can expose corruption, unravel lies, and poke fun at self-important figures of authority.
lore.features.0.description: You gain proficiency with three skills of your choice.
lore.features.0.name: Дополнительные владения
lore.features.1.description: You learn to use your wit to supernaturally distract, confuse, and otherwise sap the confidence and competence of others. When a creature that you can see within 60 feet of yourself makes a damage roll or succeeds on an ability check or attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration; roll your Bardic Inspiration die, and subtract the number rolled from the creature's roll, reducing the damage or potentially turning the success into a failure.
lore.features.1.name: Cutting Words
lore.features.2.description:
You learn two spells of your choice. These spells can come from any spell list, and they must be of a level you can cast, as shown on the Bard Features table. They count as Bard spells for you but don't count against the number of spells you know.

Whenever you gain a Bard level, you can replace one of these spells with another spell of the same requirements.
lore.features.2.name: Magical Discoveries
lore.features.3.description: When you make an ability check or attack roll and fail, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration; roll the Bardic Inspiration die, and add the number rolled to the {@dice d20}, potentially turning a failure into a success. If the check still fails, the Bardic Inspiration isn't expended.
lore.features.3.name: Peerless Skill
lore.name: Коллегия Знаний
lore.shortDescription: Plumb the Depths of Magical Knowledge
lunar.description: On many worlds, the moon is a revered celestial body with magical properties. On Krynn, the gods of magic are associated with the world's three moons. On the world of Toril, the god Selûne uses the light of the moon to battle darkness. On Eberron, scholars of the Draconic Prophecy decipher ancient secrets from the waxing and waning of that world's twelve moons. You or someone from your lineage has been exposed to the concentrated magic of the moon (or moons) of your world, imbuing you with lunar magic. Perhaps your ancestor was involved in a druidic ritual involving an eclipse, or maybe a mystical fragment of a moon crashed near you. However you came to have your magic, your connection to the moon is obvious when you cast sorcerer spells—perhaps making your pupils glow with the color of a moon from your world, causing spectral manifestations of lunar phases to orbit you, or some other effect.
lunar.features.0.description: You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown on the Lunar Spells table. Each of these spells counts as a sorcerer spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of sorcerer spells you know. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can choose what lunar phase manifests its power through your magic: Full Moon, New Moon, or Crescent Moon. While in the chosen phase, you can cast one 1st-level spell of the associated phase in the Lunar Spells table once without expending a spell slot. Once you cast a spell in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
lunar.features.0.name: Lunar Embodiment
lunar.features.1.description: You can call down the radiant light of the moon on command. You learn the {@spell Sacred Flame|XPHB} spell, which doesn't count against the number of sorcerer cantrips you know. When you cast the spell, you can target one creature as normal or target two creatures within range that are within 5 feet of each other.
lunar.features.1.name: Moon Fire
lunar.features.2.description:
The current phase of your Lunar Embodiment can affect your Metamagic feature. Each Lunar Embodiment phase is associated with certain schools of magic, as shown here:

**Full Moon.** Abjuration and Divination spells

**New Moon.** Enchantment and Necromancy spells

**Crescent Moon.** Illusion and Transmutation spells

Whenever you use Metamagic on a spell of a school of magic associated with your current Lunar Embodiment phase, you can reduce the Sorcery Points spent by 1 (minimum 0). You can reduce the Sorcery Points spent for your Metamagic a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
lunar.features.2.name: Lunar Boons
lunar.features.3.description:
You gain greater control over the phases of your lunar magic. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to change your current Lunar Embodiment phase to a different one.

You can now cast one 1st-level spell from each lunar phase of the Lunar Spells table once without expending a spell slot, provided your current phase is the same as the lunar phase spell. Once you cast a lunar phase spell in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
lunar.features.3.name: Waxing and Waning
lunar.features.4.description:
The power of a lunar phase saturates your being. While you are in a Lunar Embodiment phase, you also gain the following benefit associated with that phase:

{@b Full Moon.} You can use a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to shed {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 10-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 10 feet or to douse the light. In addition, you and creatures of your choice have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) and Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks while within the bright light you shed.

{@b New Moon.} You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks. In addition, while you are entirely in darkness, attack rolls have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} against you.

{@b Crescent Moon.} You have Resistance to Necrotic and Radiant damage.
lunar.features.4.name: Lunar Empowerment
lunar.features.5.description:
As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can tap into a special power of your current Lunar Embodiment phase. Alternatively, as part of the {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} you take to change your lunar phase using the Waxing and Waning feature, you can immediately use the power of the lunar phase you are entering.

{@b Full Moon.} You radiate moonlight for a moment. Each creature of your choice within 30 feet of you must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or have the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn. In addition, one creature of your choice in that area regains {@dice 3d8} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

{@b New Moon.} You momentarily emanate gloom. Each creature of your choice within 30 feet of you must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC or take {@dice 3d10} Necrotic damage and have its Speed reduced to 0 until the end of its next turn. In addition, you gain the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn, or until immediately after you make an attack roll or cast a spell.

{@b Crescent Moon.} You can magically teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 60 feet of yourself. You can bring along one willing creature you can see within 5 feet of yourself. That creature teleports to an unoccupied space of your choice that you can see within 5 feet of your destination space. In addition, you and that creature gain Resistance to all damage until the start of your next turn.

Once you use one of these {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} benefits, you can't use that benefit again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, unless you spend 5 Sorcery Points to use it again.
lunar.features.5.name: Lunar Phenomenon
lunar.name: Лунная Магия
lunar.shortDescription: Channel the Magic of the Moon
mastermind.description: Your focus is on people and on the influence and secrets they have. Many spies, courtiers, and schemers follow this archetype, leading lives of intrigue. Words are your weapons as often as knives or poison, and secrets and favors are some of your favorite treasures.
mastermind.features.0.description: You gain proficiency with the {@item Disguise Kit|XPHB}, the {@item Forgery Kit|XPHB}, and one {@filter Gaming Set|items|type=gaming set} of your choice. You also learn two languages of your choice. Additionally, you can unerringly mimic the speech patterns and accent of a creature that you hear speak for at least 1 minute, enabling you to pass yourself off as a native speaker of a particular land, provided that you know the language.
mastermind.features.0.name: Master of Intrigue
mastermind.features.1.description: You can use the {@action Help|XPHB} action as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}. Additionally, when you use the Help action to aid an ally in attacking a creature, the target of that attack can be within 30 feet of you, rather than within 5 feet of you, if the target can see or hear you.
mastermind.features.1.name: Master of Tactics
mastermind.name: Интриган
mercy.description: Warriors of Mercy manipulate the life force of others. These Monks are wandering physicians, but they bring a swift end to their enemies. They often wear masks, presenting themselves as faceless bringers of life and death.
mercy.features.0.description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} and deal damage, you can expend 1 Focus Point to deal extra Necrotic damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die plus your Wisdom modifier.
mercy.features.0.name: Hand of Harm
mercy.features.1.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend 1 Focus Point to touch a creature and restore a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to a roll of your Martial Arts die plus your Wisdom modifier. When you use your Flurry of Blows, you can replace one of the {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} with a use of this feature without expending a Focus Point for the healing.
mercy.features.1.name: Hand of Healing
mercy.features.2.description: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Insight} and {@skill Medicine} skills and proficiency with the {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB}.
mercy.features.2.name: Implements of Mercy
mercy.features.3.description: Your Hand of Harm and Hand of Healing improve.
mercy.features.3.details.handOfHarm: {@b Hand of Harm.} When you use Hand of Harm on a creature, you can also give that creature the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn.
mercy.features.3.details.handOfHealing: {@b Hand of Healing.} When you use Hand of Healing, you can also end one of the following conditions on the creature you heal: {@condition Blinded|XPHB}, {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, or {@condition Stunned|XPHB}.
mercy.features.3.name: Physician's Touch
mercy.features.4.description: When you use Flurry of Blows, you can replace each of the {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} with a use of Hand of Healing without expending Focus Points for the healing. In addition, when you make an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} with Flurry of Blows and deal damage, you can use Hand of Harm with that strike without expending a Focus Point for Hand of Harm. You can still use Hand of Harm only once per turn. You can use these benefits a total number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
mercy.features.4.name: Flurry of Healing and Harm
mercy.features.5.description: Your mastery of life energy opens the door to the ultimate mercy. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can touch the corpse of a creature that died within the past 24 hours and expend 5 Focus Points. The creature then returns to life with a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 4d10} plus your Wisdom modifier. If the creature died with any of the following conditions, the creature revives without them: {@condition Blinded|XPHB}, {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, and {@condition Stunned|XPHB}. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
mercy.features.5.name: Hand of Ultimate Mercy
mercy.name: Воин Милосердия
mercy.shortDescription: Manipulate Forces of Life and Death
misfortune-bringer.description: You've matched your penchant for illicit activities with the ability to mark your enemies for mishap and misfortune. Whether you were imbued with magic by spending time among the Fey or you learned the art of cursing from a long line of hedge Wizards, you are a Misfortune Bringer. Although not universal, many Misfortune Bringers possess heterochromia iridum—eyes of two different colors—and they use only one when glaring at targets they intend to curse.
misfortune-bringer.features.0.descriptio: You can place a minor curse with a glance. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself to be cursed by your Evil Eye. While a creature is cursed by your Evil Eye, you can deal Sneak Attack damage to the creature if you don't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the attack roll. The creature remains cursed by your Evil Eye for 1 minute or until you curse a different creature with your Evil Eye, whichever comes first.
misfortune-bringer.features.0.name: Evil Eye
misfortune-bringer.features.1.descriptio: You learn Misfortunes that you can inflict on those cursed by your Evil Eye.
misfortune-bringer.features.1.details.ji: You have 4 Jinx Points. You gain 2 additional Jinx Points at Rogue level 13. To use a Misfortune option, you must spend the number of Jinx Points that it costs. You regain all expended Jinx Points when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
misfortune-bringer.features.1.details.mi: You learn two Misfortunes of your choice, which are detailed under 'Misfortunes' below. You learn an additional Misfortune of your choice when you reach Rogue levels 9, 13, and 17. When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can replace one Misfortune you know with a different one.
misfortune-bringer.features.1.details.sa: If a Misfortune requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 + your Charisma or Intelligence modifier (your choice) + your Proficiency Bonus.
misfortune-bringer.features.1.name: Misfortunist
misfortune-bringer.name: Вестник Несчастья
misfortune-bringer.shortDescription: Curse Those You're About to Strike
monster-slayer.description: You have dedicated yourself to hunting down creatures of the night and wielders of grim magic. A Monster Slayer seeks out vampires, dragons, evil fey, fiends, and other magical threats. Trained in supernatural techniques to overcome such monsters, slayers are experts at unearthing and defeating mighty, mystical foes.
monster-slayer.features.0.description: Starting at 3rd level, you learn an additional spell when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown in the Monster Slayer Spells table. The spell counts as a ranger spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of ranger spells you know.
monster-slayer.features.0.name: Monster Slayer Magic
monster-slayer.features.1.description: At 3rd level, you gain the ability to peer at a creature and magically discern how best to hurt it. As an action, choose one creature you can see within 60 feet of you. You immediately learn whether the creature has any damage immunities, resistances, or vulnerabilities and what they are. If the creature is hidden from divination magic, you sense that it has no damage immunities, resistances, or vulnerabilities. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses of it when you finish a long rest.
monster-slayer.features.1.name: Hunter's Sense
monster-slayer.features.2.description: Starting at 3rd level, you can focus your ire on one foe, increasing the harm you inflict on it. As a bonus action, you designate one creature you can see within 60 feet of you as the target of this feature. The first time each turn that you hit that target with a weapon attack, it takes an extra 1d6 damage from the weapon. This benefit lasts until you finish a short or long rest. It ends early if you designate a different creature.
monster-slayer.features.2.name: Slayer's Prey
monster-slayer.features.3.description: At 7th level, you gain extra resilience against your prey's assaults on your mind and body. Whenever the target of your Slayer's Prey forces you to make a saving throw and whenever you make an ability check to escape that target's grapple, add 1d6 to your roll.
monster-slayer.features.3.name: Supernatural Defense
monster-slayer.features.4.description: At 11th level, you gain the ability to thwart someone else's magic. When you see a creature casting a spell or teleporting within 60 feet of you, you can use your reaction to try to magically foil it. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC, or its spell or teleport fails and is wasted. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.
monster-slayer.features.4.name: Magic-User's Nemesis
monster-slayer.features.5.description: At 15th level, you gain the ability to counterattack when your prey tries to sabotage you. If the target of your Slayer's Prey forces you to make a saving throw, you can use your reaction to make one weapon attack against the quarry. You make this attack immediately before making the saving throw. If your attack hits, your save automatically succeeds, in addition to the attack's normal effects.
monster-slayer.features.5.name: Slayer's Counter
monster-slayer.name: Истребитель Монстров
monster-slayer.shortDescription: Hunt Down Creatures of the Night
moon.description: Druids of the Circle of the Moon draw on lunar magic to transform themselves. Their order gathers under the moon to share news and perform rituals. Changeable as the moon, a Druid of this circle might prowl as a great cat one night, soar over the treetops as an eagle the next day, and then crash through undergrowth as a bear to drive off a trespassing monster. The wild is in the Druid's blood.
moon.features.0.description: When you reach a Druid level specified in the Circle of the Moon Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. In addition, you can cast the spells from this feature while you're in a Wild Shape form.
moon.features.0.name: Circle of the Moon Spells
moon.features.1.description: You can channel lunar magic when you assume a Wild Shape form, granting you the benefits below. {@b Challenge Rating.} The maximum {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} for the form equals your Druid level divided by 3 (round down). {@b Armor Class.} Until you leave the form, your AC equals 13 plus your Wisdom modifier if that total is higher than the Beast's AC. {@b Temporary Hit Points.} You gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to three times your Druid level.
moon.features.1.name: Circle Forms
moon.features.2.description: While in a Wild Shape form, you gain the following benefits. {@b Lunar Radiance.} Each of your attacks in a Wild Shape form can deal its normal damage type or Radiant damage. You make this choice each time you hit with those attacks. {@b Increased Toughness.} You can add your Wisdom modifier to your Constitution saving throws.
moon.features.2.name: Improved Circle Forms
moon.features.3.description: You magically transport yourself, reappearing amid a burst of moonlight. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see, and you have {@status Advantage} on the next attack roll you make before the end of this turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also regain uses by expending a level 2+ spell slot for each use you want to restore (no action required).
moon.features.3.name: Moonlight Step
moon.features.4.description: The power of the moon suffuses you, granting you the following benefits. {@b Improved Lunar Radiance.} Once on each of your turns, you can deal an extra {@damage 2d10} Radiant damage to a target you hit with a Wild Shape form's attack. {@b Shared Moonlight.} Whenever you use Moonlight Step, you can also teleport one willing creature. That creature must be within 10 feet of you, and you teleport it to an unoccupied space you can see within 10 feet of your destination space.
moon.features.4.name: Lunar Form
moon.name: Круг Луны
moon.shortDescription: Adopt Animal Forms to Guard the Wilds
mutation.description: Druids of the Circle of Mutation believe that nature should be improved in order to compete with the unnatural world. Their order hides in the darkest corners of swamps and forests, conducting experiments to warp the structure of their domain. They go unheard for months, emerging from the wilds with twisted vines and mutated creatures at their side. These Druids have earned the scorn of other circles, discredited as those who have lost their way. In the eyes of these Druids, those who wish to preserve nature as it is simply fear what change brings.
mutation.features.0.description: You channel the endless possibilities of nature when you assume a Wild Shape form, granting you the benefits below. {@b Challenge Rating.} The maximum {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} for the form equals your Druid level divided by 3 (rounded down). {@b Predator's Strike.} When you hit with an attack roll using a Beast form's attack in Wild Shape, you add +2 to the damage dealt. {@b Unpredictable.} You can take the {@action Dash|XPHB}, {@action Disengage|XPHB}, or {@action Influence|XPHB} action as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}.
mutation.features.0.name: Circle Forms
mutation.features.1.description: When you assume a Wild Shape form, or as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} while your Wild Shape is active, you can expend a spell slot to gain Mutation Points equal to the slot's level. These Mutation Points last until they are spent, you gain additional Mutation Points, or you leave the form. While you have Mutation Points, you can spend them on your turn (no action required by you) to gain a Mutation from the list below. When you do, your body distends and reconstitutes in a gruesome display. Mutations last until you leave the form or you expend a spell slot to gain Mutation Points.
mutation.features.1.details.creatureOfTh: {@b Creature of the Sea.} Cost: 1 Mutation Point. You gain a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and you can breathe underwater. For an additional 1 Mutation Point, you gain a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to twice your speed.
{@b Creature of the Sky.} Cost: 3 Mutation Points. You gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet. For an additional 2 Mutation Points, your {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} increases to 60 feet.
mutation.features.1.details.darkvision: {@b Darkvision.} Cost: 1 Mutation Point. You gain {@sense Darkvision} with a range of 60 feet. If you already have {@sense Darkvision}, its range increases by 60 feet.
mutation.features.1.details.disquietingV: {@b Disquieting Voice.} Cost: 1 Mutation Point. You can comprehend and verbally communicate with Beasts, and you can use any of the {@action Influence|XPHB} action's skill options with them.
mutation.features.1.details.echolocation: {@b Echolocation.} Cost: 3 Mutation Points. You have {@sense Blindsight} with a range of 30 feet while you don't have the {@condition Deafened} condition.
mutation.features.1.details.enlarge: {@b Enlarge.} Cost: 2 Mutation Points. Your size increases by one category (from Medium to Large, for example). You can spend an additional 2 Mutation Points to increase your size by two categories instead (from Medium to Huge, for example), gaining the benefits of the spell again. See the {@spell Enlarge/Reduce} spell to determine the benefits gained from this ability.
mutation.features.1.details.manipulateOb: {@b Manipulate Objects.} Cost: 1 Mutation Point. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can take the {@action Utilize|XPHB} action.
mutation.features.1.details.multiattack: {@b Multiattack.} Cost: 2 Mutation Points. You can attack twice with a Wild Shape form's attack instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.
mutation.features.1.details.mutationOpti: The following options are available to your Mutate Shape feature. The options are presented in alphabetical order.
mutation.features.1.details.naturalArmor: {@b Natural Armor.} Cost: 2 Mutation Points. You gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}. {@b Repeatable.} You can gain this Mutation more than once, but no more than three times.
mutation.features.1.details.reduce: {@b Reduce.} Cost: 1 Mutation Point. Your size decreases by one category (from Medium to Small, for example). You can spend an additional 2 Mutation Points to decrease your size by two categories instead (from Medium to Tiny, for example). See the {@spell Enlarge/Reduce} spell to determine the benefits gained from this ability.
mutation.features.1.details.venomousAtta: {@b Venomous Attacks.} Cost: 2 Mutation Points. Once per turn, a creature you hit with a Wild Shape form's attack takes an extra {@dice 2d6} Poison damage, and you can force a creature you hit with a Wild Shape form's attack to make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature is {@condition Poisoned} for 1 minute. It can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the {@condition Poisoned} condition on a success. {@b Repeatable.} You can gain this Mutation more than once, but no more than three times. For each additional 2 Mutation points, the attack does an additional {@dice 2d6} Poison damage.
mutation.features.1.details.wallCrawler: {@b Wall Crawler.} Cost: 1 Mutation Point. You gain a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your speed. For an additional 1 Mutation Point, you gain a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to twice your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
mutation.features.1.name: Mutate Shape
mutation.features.2.description: When you expend a level 2+ spell slot to gain Mutation Points, you regain one expended spell slot. The slot you regain must be of a level lower than the slot you expended and can't be higher than level 5. In addition, while in Wild Shape form, you gain the following benefits. {@b Unnatural Attacks.} Each of your attacks in Wild Shape form can deal its normal damage type or Force damage. You make this choice each time you hit with those attacks. {@b Unnerving Aura.} You have {@status Advantage} on Charisma ({@skill Deception} or {@skill Intimidation}) and Wisdom ({@skill Animal Handling}) checks.
mutation.features.2.name: Unnatural and Unnerving
mutation.features.3.description: When you gain Mutation Points, you gain an additional number of Mutation Points equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1). In addition, you can now spend your Mutation Points on the following Mutations.
mutation.features.3.details.creatureOfEa: {@b Creature of Earth.} Cost: 3 Mutation Points. You have {@sense Tremorsense} with a range of 30 feet. You gain a {@variantrule Burrow Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
mutation.features.3.details.eldritchAssa: {@b Eldritch Assault.} Cost: 2 Mutation Points. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls you make with your Wild Shape form's attacks. {@b Repeatable.} You can gain this Mutation more than once, but no more than three times.
mutation.features.3.details.elementalHar: {@b Elemental Harness.} Cost: 2 Mutation Points. You gain Resistance to one of the following damage types of your choice: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder. {@b Repeatable.} You can gain this Mutation more than once, but you must choose a different Resistance each time.
mutation.features.3.details.mysticMonste: {@b Mystic Monster.} Cost: 3 Mutation Points. If you have 18 or more Druid levels, creatures have {@status Disadvantage} on saving throws against spells you cast while this Mutation is active.
mutation.features.3.details.rapidRegener: {@b Rapid Regeneration.} Cost: 5 Mutation Points. At the start of each of your turns, you regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1 {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB} regained).
mutation.features.3.details.supernatural: {@b Supernatural Hide.} Cost: 5 Mutation Points. You have Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.
mutation.features.3.name: Endless Evolution
mutation.features.4.description: Your mastery of mutation has made you an apex predator, granting you the following benefits. {@b Evolved Attacks.} Once per turn, you can deal an extra {@damage 2d10} Force damage to a target you hit with a Wild Shape form's attack. {@b Mutate Beasts.} As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can touch a Beast and expend a spell slot, causing the target to mutate. You gain Mutation Points equal to the slot's level, which you must immediately spend on Mutations for the Beast. Unspent Mutation Points are lost. Mutations remain until the Beast is targeted by this feature again. A {@spell Remove Curse}, {@spell Greater Restoration} or similar magic ends the Mutations.
mutation.features.4.name: Apex Predator
mutation.name: Круг Мутации
nightwatcher.description: Nightwatchers come from humble origins, often toiling as guards or investigators. Undaunted by dangers in the darkness, they stand firm against the growing night. Ever vigilant, they use the darkness to surprise and overwhelm foes much greater than themselves.
nightwatcher.features.0.description: Unraveling mysteries under the cover of night has honed your skills. You gain the following benefits.
nightwatcher.features.0.details.expertis: {@b Expertise.} Choose one of your skill proficiencies with which you lack {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB}. You gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in that skill.
nightwatcher.features.0.details.skilled: {@b Skilled.} You gain proficiency in two skills of your choice from the following list: {@skill Deception}, {@skill History}, {@skill Insight}, {@skill Intimidation}, {@skill Investigation}, {@skill Perception}, or {@skill Stealth}.
nightwatcher.features.0.name: Skilled Guardian
nightwatcher.features.1.description: Your long nights of vigilance have left your senses attuned to signs of danger, granting you the following benefits.
nightwatcher.features.1.details.darkvisi: {@b Darkvision.} You gain {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet. If you already have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} when you gain this feature, its range increases by 60 feet.
nightwatcher.features.1.details.keenSens: {@b Keen Senses.} You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} rolls and Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks.
nightwatcher.features.1.details.warningS: {@b Warning Shout.} When you make an {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to warn creatures of your choice within 30 feet of yourself that can see or hear you. Each creature can then take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on its {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} roll and move up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
nightwatcher.features.1.name: Ever Vigilant
nightwatcher.features.2.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you assess a creature you can see within 30 feet of you. Until the start of your next turn, when that creature makes an attack roll against you or another creature within 5 feet of you, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on that roll and give {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to that damage.
nightwatcher.features.2.name: Size Up
nightwatcher.features.3.description: You are adept at tracking down enemies in the dead of night, giving you these benefits.
nightwatcher.features.3.details.blindsig: {@b Blindsight.} As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you gain {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 30 feet for 10 minutes. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
nightwatcher.features.3.details.slippery: {@b Slippery.} {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} against you. While entirely within {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, your movement doesn't provoke {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}.
nightwatcher.features.3.name: Night Stalker
nightwatcher.features.4.description: When you make an {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} roll, you can treat a {@dice d20} roll of 9 or lower as a 10. When you roll 18–20 on an {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} roll, you can take one additional action, except the {@action Magic|XPHB} action, on your first turn.
nightwatcher.features.4.name: Ready for Action
nightwatcher.features.5.description: Immediately after you miss a creature under the effect of your Size Up feature with an attack roll, you can take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} or {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make a melee attack against that creature if it's within range. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the new attack roll against that creature.
nightwatcher.features.5.name: Beat Down
nightwatcher.name: Ночной Дозорный
noble-genie.description: Paladins sworn to the Oath of the Noble Genies revere the forces of the Elemental Planes. Through taking this oath, Paladins draw power from the four different types of genies—dao, masters of earth, djinn, masters of air, efreet, masters of fire, and marids, masters of water. In Faerûn, many Paladins who swear this oath hail from Calimshan, a land teeming with genies.
noble-genie.features.0.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of the Noble Genies Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
noble-genie.features.0.name: Oath of the Noble Genies Spells
noble-genie.features.1.description: Immediately after you cast {@spell Divine Smite|XPHB}, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity and invoke one of the following effects.
noble-genie.features.1.details.daosCrush: {@b Dao's Crush.} Earth rises up around the target of your {@spell Divine Smite|XPHB}. The target has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape DC equal to your spell save DC). While {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, the target has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition.
noble-genie.features.1.details.djinnisEs: {@b Djinni's Escape.} You teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of yourself and take on a semi-incorporeal form, which lasts until the end of your next turn. While in this form, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage, and you have Immunity to the {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, {@condition Prone|XPHB}, and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions.
noble-genie.features.1.details.efreetisF: {@b Efreeti's Fury.} The target of your {@spell Divine Smite|XPHB} takes an extra {@damage 2d4} Fire damage, and fire jumps from the target to another creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. The second creature also takes {@damage 2d4} Fire damage.
noble-genie.features.1.details.maridsSur: {@b Marid's Surge.} The target of your {@spell Divine Smite|XPHB} and each creature of your choice in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} originating from you make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature is pushed 15 feet straight away from you and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.
noble-genie.features.1.name: Elemental Smite
noble-genie.features.2.description: When you aren't wearing any armor, your base Armor Class equals 10 plus your Dexterity and Charisma modifiers. You can use a {@item Shield|XPHB} and still gain this benefit. You also gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: {@skill Acrobatics}, {@skill Intimidation}, {@skill Performance}, or {@skill Persuasion}.
noble-genie.features.2.name: Genie's Splendor
noble-genie.features.3.description: Choose one of the following damage types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. You and your allies have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to that damage type while in your Aura of Protection. At the start of each of your turns, you can change the damage type affected by this feature to one of the other listed options (no action required).
noble-genie.features.3.name: Aura of Elemental Shielding
noble-genie.features.4.description: When you are hit by an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to halve the attack's damage against yourself (round down) and force the attacker to make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the attacker takes damage equal to {@damage 2d10} plus your Charisma modifier of one of the following types (your choice): Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. On a successful save, the attacker takes half as much damage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
noble-genie.features.4.name: Elemental Rebuke
noble-genie.features.5.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you gain the benefits below for 10 minutes or until you end them (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). {@b Flight.} You have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 60 feet and can hover. {@b Minor Wish.} When you or an ally in your Aura of Protection fails a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make the {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} succeed instead.
noble-genie.features.5.name: Noble Scion
noble-genie.name: Клятва Благородных Джиннов
occultist.description: В Этарисе есть те, кто боится магии, и те, кто стремится её понять. Охотники на монстров из Гильдии Оккультистов знают, что и те, и другие одинаково опасны. Оккультист исследует тайное, сверхъестественное и потустороннее, избегая влияния суеверий и идеологий. Несмотря на репутацию охотников на магов, Оккультисты не сжигают своих врагов на кострах — Оккультисты пускают их под меч, потому что мечи для этого и существуют. Знания и магия дают Оккультистам преимущество над их мистическими противниками. Их заклинания используются для обнаружения и защиты от опасных арканных сил, а главное — магия используется для уничтожения монстров, которым не повредить сталью.
occultist.features.0.name: Адепт оккультного
occultist.features.1.description: Ваше изучение оккультного даёт вам способность сотворять заклинания.
occultist.features.1.details.cantrips: Вы изучаете два заговора на ваш выбор из {@filter списка заклинаний Волшебника|spells|class=Wizard}. Каждый раз, получая уровень Охотника на монстров, вы можете заменить один из этих заговоров другим на ваш выбор из {@filter списка заклинаний Волшебника|spells|class=Wizard}. Когда вы достигаете 10-го уровня Охотника на монстров, вы изучаете ещё один заговор {@filter Волшебника|spells|class=Wizard} на ваш выбор.
occultist.features.1.details.changingPre: Каждый раз, получая уровень Охотника на монстров, вы можете заменить одно заклинание из своего списка другим {@filter заклинанием Волшебника|spells|class=Wizard}.
occultist.features.1.details.preparedSpe: Вы готовите список заклинаний 1-го уровня и выше, доступных для сотворения этим умением. Для начала выберите три заклинания {@filter Волшебника|spells|class=Wizard} 1-го уровня. Рекомендуются {@spell Burning Hands|XPHB|Огненные ладони}, {@spell Detect Magic|XPHB|Обнаружение магии} и {@spell Protection from Evil and Good|XPHB|Защита от зла и добра}. Количество заклинаний в вашем списке увеличивается по мере получения уровней Охотника на монстров, как показано в столбце «Подготовленные заклинания» таблицы «Сотворение заклинаний Оккультиста». Каждый раз, когда это число увеличивается, выбирайте дополнительные заклинания из {@filter списка заклинаний Волшебника|spells|class=Wizard}, пока количество заклинаний в вашем списке не сравняется с числом в таблице. Выбранные заклинания должны быть уровня, для которого у вас есть ячейки заклинаний.
occultist.features.1.details.spellSlots: В таблице «Сотворение заклинаний Оккультиста» показано, сколько у вас ячеек заклинаний для сотворения заклинаний 1-го уровня и выше. Вы восстанавливаете все потраченные ячейки заклинаний, когда завершаете {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB|Долгий отдых}.
occultist.features.1.details.spellcastin: Интеллект — ваша заклинательная характеристика для заклинаний {@filter Волшебника|spells|class=Wizard}.
Вы можете использовать {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB|Магическую фокусировку} как {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB|Заклинательную фокусировку} для заклинаний {@filter Волшебника|spells|class=Wizard}.
occultist.features.1.name: Использование заклинаний
occultist.features.2.description: Вы совершаете с {@status Advantage|XPHB|Преимуществом} спасброски против заклинаний, сотворённых типами существ из вашего Гримуара монстров. Кроме того, когда видимое вами существо в пределах 60 футов сотворяет заклинание или совершает атаку заклинанием, вы можете использовать Изученный отклик против этого существа до того, как заклинание будет сотворено.
occultist.features.2.name: Магическое вмешательство
occultist.features.3.description: Вы считаете Гуманоидов, способных сотворять заклинания, типом существ из вашего Гримуара монстров. Кроме того, когда вы наносите урон типу существа из вашего Гримуара монстров, который {@status Concentrating|XPHB|концентрируется}, оно совершает с {@status Disadvantage|XPHB|Помехой} спасбросок для поддержания {@status Concentration|XPHB|Концентрации}.
occultist.features.3.name: Охотник на магов
occultist.features.4.description: Ваши знания магии возросли настолько, что вы научились сотворять Ритуалы. Вы можете сотворять любое заклинание как Ритуал, если у этого заклинания есть тег Ритуал и оно есть среди подготовленных вами заклинаний. Кроме того, вы изучаете два заклинания на ваш выбор. Эти заклинания могут быть из {@filter списка заклинаний Жреца|spells|class=Cleric}, {@filter Друида|spells|class=Druid} или {@filter Волшебника|spells|class=Wizard} или их любого сочетания. У выбранного заклинания должен быть тег Ритуал. Когда вы достигаете 14-го уровня Охотника на монстров, вы можете заменить одно из заклинаний, известных вам от этого умения, другим заклинанием из любого списка. У нового заклинания должен быть тег Ритуал.
occultist.features.4.name: Оккультные знания
occultist.features.5.description: Вы получаете способность создавать защитную ауру. Вы излучаете нерушимую ауру в виде {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB|Эманации} с радиусом 20 футов, исходящую от вас. Аура неактивна, пока у вас состояние {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB|Недееспособный}. Вы и союзники в вашей ауре совершаете с {@status Advantage|XPHB|Преимуществом} спасброски против заклинаний, сотворённых типами существ из вашего Гримуара монстров. Кроме того, у вас всегда есть подготовленное заклинание {@spell Counterspell|XPHB|Контрзаклинание}. Вы можете сотворить {@spell Counterspell|XPHB|Контрзаклинание} один раз, не тратя ячейку заклинания. Сотворив заклинание этим умением, вы не можете делать так снова, пока не завершите {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB|Долгий отдых}.
occultist.features.5.name: Магическая эгида
occultist.features.6.description: Вы научились предугадывать врагов настолько хорошо, что можете быстро сотворять заклинания в ответ на их атаки. Когда вы используете Изученный отклик, вы можете сотворить заклинание. Это заклинание должно иметь время сотворения «действие» и должно быть нацелено только на это существо.
occultist.features.6.name: Магический отклик
occultist.name: Гильдия Оккультистов
occultist.shortDescription: Охотьтесь на магов и магических противников
open-hand.description: Warriors of the Open Hand are masters of unarmed combat. They learn techniques to push and trip their opponents and channel their own energy to protect themselves from harm.
open-hand.features.0.description: Whenever you hit a creature with an attack granted by your Flurry of Blows, you can impose one of the following effects on that target.
open-hand.features.0.details.addle: {@b Addle.} The target can't make {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} until the start of its next turn.
open-hand.features.0.details.push: {@b Push.} The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 15 feet away from you.
open-hand.features.0.details.topple: {@b Topple.} The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.
open-hand.features.0.name: Open Hand Technique
open-hand.features.1.description: You gain the ability to heal yourself. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can roll your Martial Arts die. You regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the number rolled plus your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1 {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB}). You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
open-hand.features.1.name: Wholeness of Body
open-hand.features.2.description: When you take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} other than Step of the Wind, you can also use Step of the Wind immediately after that {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}.
open-hand.features.2.name: Fleet Step
open-hand.features.3.description: You gain the ability to set up lethal vibrations in someone's body. When you hit a creature with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can expend 4 Focus Points to start these imperceptible vibrations, which last for a number of days equal to your Monk level. The vibrations are harmless unless you take an action to end them. Alternatively, when you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can replace one of the attacks with this action. To use this action, you and the target must be on the same plane of existence. When you use this action, the target must make a Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 10d12} Force damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. You can have only one creature under the effect of this feature at a time. You can end the vibrations harmlessly (no action required).
open-hand.features.3.name: Quivering Palm
open-hand.name: Воин Открытой Ладони
order.description: The Order Domain represents discipline, as well as devotion to a society or an institution and strict obedience to the laws governing it. On Ravnica, the domain is favored by clerics of the Azorius Senate, who use it to maintain and enforce the law, and of the Orzhov Syndicate, who exploit law and order for their personal gain. On other worlds, gods who grant access to this domain include {@deity Bane}, {@deity Tyr}, {@deity Majere}, {@deity Erathis}, {@deity Pholtus}, {@deity Wee Jas}, {@deity Aureon}, {@deity Maglubiyet}, {@deity Nuada}, {@deity Athena}, {@deity Anubis}, {@deity Forseti}, and {@deity Asmodeus}. The ideal of order is obedience to the law above all else, rather than to a specific individual or the passing influence of emotion or popular rule. Clerics of order are typically concerned with how things are done, rather than whether an action's results are just.
