trick-shot.features.4.description: Один раз в каждый ваш ход, когда вы попадаете по существу дальнобойной атакой оружием, вы можете отрикошетить снаряд по дополнительным целям. Выберите другую цель в пределах 30 футов от первой и совершите бросок атаки против неё. При попадании вы можете повторить эту атаку по новой цели в пределах 30 футов, пока не промахнётесь или не совершите всего пять атак. Вы не можете нацелить одно и то же существо более чем одной атакой каждый раз, когда используете это умение. Использовав это умение, вы не можете использовать его снова, пока не завершите {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Короткий отдых} или {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB|Долгий отдых}. Вы также можете восстановить его использование, потратив две Кости риска (действие не требуется).
trick-shot.features.4.name: Выстрел-пинбол
trick-shot.name: Трюкач
trickery.description: The Trickery Domain offers magic of deception, illusion, and stealth. Clerics who wield this magic are a disruptive force in the world, puncturing pride, mocking tyrants, freeing captives, and flouting hollow traditions. They prefer subterfuge and pranks to direct confrontation. Gods of trickery are mischief-makers and instigators who stand as a constant challenge to the accepted order among both gods and mortals. They embody the forces of change and social upheaval, and they're patrons of thieves, scoundrels, gamblers, rebels, and liberators. Religious orders that operate in secret, especially those that seek to undermine oppressive governments or hierarchies, also draw on the power of the Trickery Domain. Make Mischief and Challenge Authority.
trickery.features.0.description: Your connection to this divine domain ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach a Cleric level specified in the Trickery Domain Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
trickery.features.0.name: Trickery Domain Spells
trickery.features.1.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can choose yourself or a willing creature within 30 feet of yourself to have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks. This blessing lasts until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} or you use this feature again.
trickery.features.1.name: Blessing of the Trickster
trickery.features.2.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to create a perfect visual illusion of yourself in an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of yourself. The illusion is intangible and doesn't occupy its space. It lasts for 1 minute, but it ends early if you dismiss it (no action required) or have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. The illusion is animated and mimics your expressions and gestures. While it persists, you gain the following benefits: {@b Cast Spells.} You can cast spells as though you were in the illusion's space, but you must use your own senses. {@b Distract.} When both you and your illusion are within 5 feet of a creature that can see the illusion, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against that creature, given how distracting the illusion is to the target. {@b Move.} As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can move the illusion up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see that is within 120 feet of yourself.
trickery.features.2.name: Invoke Duplicity
trickery.features.3.description: Whenever you take the {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to create or move the illusion of your Invoke Duplicity, you can teleport, swapping places with the illusion.
trickery.features.3.name: Trickster's Transposition
trickery.features.4.description: The illusion of your Invoke Duplicity has grown more powerful in the following ways. {@b Shared Distraction.} When you and your allies make attack rolls against a creature within 5 feet of the illusion, the attack rolls have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}. {@b Healing Illusion.} When the illusion ends, you or a creature of your choice within 5 feet of it regains a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Cleric level.
trickery.features.4.name: Improved Duplicity
trickery.name: Домен Обмана
twilight.description: The twilit transition from light into darkness often brings calm and even joy, as the day's labors end and the hours of rest begin. The darkness can also bring terrors, but the gods of twilight guard against the horrors of the night. Clerics who serve these deities—examples of which appear on the Twilight Deities table—bring comfort to those who seek rest and protect them by venturing into the encroaching darkness to ensure that the dark is a comfort, not a terror.
twilight.features.0.description: Your connection to this divine domain ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach a Cleric level specified in the Twilight Domain Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
twilight.features.0.name: Twilight Domain Spells
twilight.features.1.description: You gain proficiency with martial weapons and heavy armor.
twilight.features.1.name: Дополнительные владения
twilight.features.2.description: You can see through the deepest gloom. You have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} out to a range of 300 feet. As an action, you can magically share this Darkvision with willing creatures you can see within 10 feet of you, up to a number of creatures equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of one creature). The shared Darkvision lasts for 1 hour. Once you share it, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, unless you expend a spell slot of any level to share it again.
twilight.features.2.name: Eyes of Night
twilight.features.3.description: The night has taught you to be vigilant. As an action, you give one creature you touch (including possibly yourself) {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the next {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} roll the creature makes. This benefit ends immediately after the roll or if you use this feature again.
twilight.features.3.name: Vigilant Blessing
twilight.features.4.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to refresh your allies with soothing twilight. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you present your {@item Holy Symbol|XPHB}, and a sphere of twilight emanates from you. The sphere is centered on you, has a 30-foot radius, and is filled with {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB}. The sphere moves with you, and it lasts for 1 minute or until you are {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} or die. Whenever a creature (including you) ends its turn in the sphere, you can grant that creature one of these benefits: You grant it {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 1d6} plus your Cleric level. You end one effect on it causing it to be {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB}.
twilight.features.4.name: Twilight Sanctuary
twilight.features.5.description: You can draw on the mystical power of night to rise into the air. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} when you are in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or darkness, you can magically grant yourself a flying speed equal to your walking speed for 1 minute. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
twilight.features.5.name: Steps of Night
twilight.features.6.description: The twilight that you summon offers a protective embrace: you and your allies have half cover while in the sphere created by your Twilight Sanctuary.
twilight.features.6.name: Twilight Shroud
twilight.name: Домен Сумерек
twilight.shortDescription: Guardian of the Twilight Between Light and Dark
undead.description:
You've made a pact with a deathless being, a creature that defies the cycle of life and death, forsaking its mortal shell so it might eternally pursue its unfathomable ambitions. For such beings, time and mortality are fleeting things, the concerns of those for whom grains of sand still rush through life's hourglass. Having once been mortal themselves, these ancient undead know firsthand the paths of ambition and the routes past the doors of death. They eagerly share this profane knowledge, along with other secrets, with those who work their will among the living.

Beings of this type include the demilich Acererak, the vampire tyrant Kas the Bloody-Handed, the githyanki lich-queen Vlaakith, the dracolich Dragotha, the undead pharaoh Ankhtepot, and the elusive Darklord, Azalin Rex.
undead.features.0.description: The Undead lets you choose from an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell. The following spells are added to the warlock spell list for you.
undead.features.0.name: Расширенный список заклинаний
undead.features.1.description: You manifest an aspect of your patron's dreadful power. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you transform for 1 minute. You gain the following benefits while transformed:
undead.features.1.details.appearance: The appearance of your Form of Dread reflects some aspect of your patron. For example, your form could be a shroud of shadows forming the crown and robes of your lich patron, or your body might glow with glyphs from ancient funerary rites and be surrounded by desert winds, suggesting your mummy patron.
undead.features.1.details.frighten: Once during each of your turns, when you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can force it to make a Wisdom saving throw, and if the saving throw fails, the target is {@status Frightened} of you until the end of your next turn.
undead.features.1.details.frightenedImmu: You are immune to the {@status Frightened} condition.
undead.features.1.details.temporaryHitPo: You gain temporary hit points equal to {@dice 1d10} + your warlock level.
undead.features.1.details.uses: You can transform a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
undead.features.1.name: Form of Dread
undead.features.2.description:
Your patron's powers have a profound effect on your body and magic. You don't need to eat, drink, or breathe.

In addition, once during each of your turns, when you hit a creature with an attack roll and roll damage against the creature, you can replace the damage type with necrotic damage. While you are using your Form of Dread, you can roll one additional damage die when determining the necrotic damage the target takes.
undead.features.2.name: Grave Touched
undead.features.3.description:
Your connection to undeath and necrotic energy now saturates your body. You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage. If you are transformed using your Form of Dread, you instead become immune to Necrotic damage.

In addition, when you would be reduced to 0 hit points, you can use your {@action Reaction|XPHB} to drop to 1 hit point instead and cause your body to erupt with deathly energy. Each creature of your choice that is within 30 feet of you takes necrotic damage equal to {@dice 2d10} + your warlock level. You then gain 1 level of {@status exhaustion}. Once you use this reaction, you can't do so again until you finish {@dice 1d4} long rests.
undead.features.3.name: Necrotic Husk
undead.features.4.description:
Your spirit can become untethered from your physical form. As an action, you can project your spirit from your body. The body you leave behind is {@status Unconscious|XPHB} and in a state of suspended animation.

Your spirit resembles your mortal form in almost every way, replicating your game statistics but not your possessions. Any damage or other effects that apply to your spirit or physical body affects the other. Your spirit can remain outside your body for up to 1 hour or until your {@variantrule Concentration|XPHB} is broken (as if concentrating on a spell). When your projection ends, your spirit returns to your body or your body magically teleports to your spirit's space (your choice).

While projecting your spirit, you gain the following benefits:
undead.features.4.details.flying: You have a flying speed equal to your walking speed and can hover. You can move through creatures and objects as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}, but you take {@dice 1d10} Force damage if you end your turn inside a creature or an object.
undead.features.4.details.necroticHealin: While you are using your Form of Dread, once during each of your turns when you deal Necrotic damage to a creature, you regain hit points equal to half the amount of Necrotic damage dealt.
undead.features.4.details.resistance: Your spirit and body gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.
undead.features.4.details.spellcasting: When you cast a spell of the Conjuration or Necromancy school, the spell doesn't require Verbal or Somatic components or Material components that lack a gold cost.
undead.features.4.details.uses: Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
undead.features.4.name: Spirit Projection
undead.name: Нежить
undying.description:
Death holds no sway over your patron, who has unlocked the secrets of everlasting life, although such a prize—like all power—comes at a price. Once mortal, the Undying has seen mortal lifetimes pass like the seasons, like the flicker of endless days and nights. It has the secrets of the ages to share, secrets of life and death. Beings of this sort include Vecna, Lord of the Hand and the Eye; the dread Iuz; the lich-queen Vol; the Undying Court of Aerenal; Vlaakith, lich-queen of the githyanki; and the deathless wizard Fistandantalus.

In the Realms, Undying patrons include Larloch the Shadow King, legendary master of Warlock's Crypt, and Gilgeam, the God-King of Unther.
undying.features.0.description: The Undying lets you choose from an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell. The following spells are added to the warlock spell list for you.
undying.features.0.name: Расширенный список заклинаний
undying.features.1.description:
You learn the {@spell Spare the Dying} cantrip, which counts as a warlock cantrip for you. You also have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against any disease.

Additionally, undead have difficulty harming you. If an undead targets you directly with an attack or a harmful spell, that creature must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC (an undead needn't make the save when it includes you in an area effect, such as the explosion of {@spell Fireball|XPHB}). On a failed save, the creature must choose a new target or forfeit targeting someone instead of you, potentially wasting the attack or spell. On a successful save, the creature is immune to this effect for 24 hours. An undead is also immune to this effect for 24 hours if you target it with an attack or a harmful spell.
undying.features.1.name: Among the Dead
undying.features.2.description:
Starting at 6th level, you can give yourself vitality when you cheat death or when you help someone else cheat it. You can regain hit points equal to {@dice 1d8} + your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1 hit point) when you succeed on a death saving throw or when you stabilize a creature with {@spell Spare the Dying}.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.
undying.features.2.name: Бросающий вызов смерти
undying.features.3.description:
You can hold your breath indefinitely, and you don't require food, water, or sleep, although you still require rest to reduce {@status exhaustion} and still benefit from finishing short and long rests.

In addition, you age at a slower rate. For every 10 years that pass, your body ages only 1 year, and you are immune to being magically aged.
undying.features.3.name: Undying Nature
undying.features.4.description:
You partake of some of the true secrets of the Undying. On your turn, you can use a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to regain hit points equal to {@dice 1d8} + your warlock level. Additionally, if you put a severed body part of yours back in place when you use this feature, the part reattaches.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
undying.features.4.name: Indestructible Life
undying.name: Бессмертный
valor.description: Bards of the College of Valor are daring skalds whose tales keep alive the memory of the great heroes of the past, and thereby inspire a new generation of heroes. These bards gather in mead halls or around great bonfires to sing the deeds of the mighty, both past and present.
valor.features.0.description:
You can use your wit to turn the tide of battle. A creature that has a Bardic Inspiration die from you can use it for one of the following effects.

When the creature is hit by an attack roll, it can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to roll the Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to its AC against that attack, potentially causing the attack to miss.

Immediately after the creature hits a target with an attack roll, the creature can roll the Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to the attack's damage against the target.
valor.features.0.name: Combat Inspiration
valor.features.1.description: You gain proficiency with {@item martial weapons|XPHB} and training with {@item Medium Armor|XPHB} and {@item Shields|XPHB}.
valor.features.1.name: Martial Training
valor.features.2.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn. In addition, you can cast one of your cantrips that has a casting time of an action in place of one of those attacks.
valor.features.2.name: Дополнительная атака
valor.features.3.description: После того как вы накладываете заклинание со временем накладывания «действие», вы можете совершить одну атаку оружием {@action Bonus Action|XPHB|Бонусным действием}.
valor.features.3.name: Battle Magic
valor.name: Коллегия Доблести
vengeance.description: The Oath of Vengeance is a solemn commitment to punish those who have committed grievously sinful acts. When evil armies slaughter helpless villagers, when a tyrant defies the will of the gods, when a thieves' guild grows too violent and powerful, when a dragon rampages through the countryside—at times like these, paladins arise and swear an Oath of Vengeance to set right that which has gone wrong.
vengeance.features.0.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of Vengeance Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
vengeance.features.0.name: Oath of Vengeance Spells
vengeance.features.1.description: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to utter a vow of enmity against a creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against the creature for 1 minute or until it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or falls {@condition Unconscious|XPHB}. If the creature drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} before the vow ends, you can transfer the vow to a different creature within 30 feet of yourself (no action required).
vengeance.features.1.name: Vow of Enmity
vengeance.features.2.description: Your supernatural focus helps you close off a foe's retreat. When you hit a creature with an {@variantrule Opportunity Attack|XPHB}, you can reduce the creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} to 0 until the end of the current turn. You can then move up to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} as part of the same {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}. This movement doesn't provoke {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}.
vengeance.features.2.name: Relentless Avenger
vengeance.features.3.description: The bond of your Vow of Enmity intensifies. When a creature under the effect of your Vow of Enmity makes an attack, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make a melee attack against that creature if it is within range.
vengeance.features.3.name: Soul of Vengeance
vengeance.features.4.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you gain the benefits below for 10 minutes or until you end them (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). {@b Flight.} You sprout spectral wings on your back, have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 60 feet, and can hover. {@b Frightful Aura.} Whenever an enemy starts its turn in your Aura of Protection, that creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute or until it takes any damage. Attack rolls against the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} creature have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.
vengeance.features.4.name: Avenging Angel
vengeance.name: Клятва Мести
vermin-lord.description: Vermin Lords cultivate loyal hordes of disease-bearing rodents to help them battle greater evils. For many of these Rangers, that greater evil is some kind of societal ill, but a rare few see their fellow Humanoids as an infestation that threatens the natural world. Regardless of their goal, they make their homes in sewers, slums, and other forgotten places where they are free to plot against their enemies and tend to their verminkin.
vermin-lord.features.0.description: You can comprehend and verbally communicate with Tiny Beasts. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend a spell slot to summon vermin swarms. You summon a number of swarms equal to the slot's level for 1 hour. Each swarm is summoned to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of yourself. A swarm uses the {@creature Swarm of Vermin} stat block. In combat, each swarm acts during your turn. It can move and use its {@action Reaction|XPHB} on its own, but the only action it takes is the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action unless you take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to command it to take an action in its stat block or some other action. You can command each swarm with a single {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
vermin-lord.features.0.name: Verminkin
vermin-lord.features.1.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can command all of your swarms to make an {@action Attack|XPHB} action instead of just one. Alternatively, a single swarm can attack twice instead of once when it takes the {@action Attack|XPHB} action. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
vermin-lord.features.1.name: Swarming Strikes
vermin-lord.features.2.description: When you reach a Ranger level specified in the Vermin Lord Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
vermin-lord.features.2.name: Vermin Lord Spells
vermin-lord.features.3.description: The time you've spent with plague-bearing rodents has rendered you Immune to the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition. Additionally, you gain proficiency in Constitution saving throws.
vermin-lord.features.3.name: Filth and Fortitude
vermin-lord.features.4.description: When you use Swarming Strikes, each swarm that takes the {@action Attack|XPHB} action makes one additional attack. Each creature damaged by a swarm's attack during this action has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn.
vermin-lord.features.4.name: Infectious Spread
vermin-lord.features.5.description: You can call on your rodent minions for defense. When you take damage from a creature you can see within 10 feet of yourself, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to direct the damage toward a swarm you control you can see within 5 feet of yourself.
vermin-lord.features.5.name: Strength of the Swarm
vermin-lord.name: Повелитель Паразитов
vermin-lord.shortDescription: Grow Powerful from Strength in Numbers
war.description:
A variety of arcane colleges specialize in training wizards for war. The tradition of War Magic blends principles of evocation and abjuration, rather than specializing in either of those schools. It teaches techniques that empower a caster's spells, while also providing methods for wizards to bolster their own defenses.

Followers of this tradition are known as war mages. They see their magic as both a weapon and armor, a resource superior to any piece of steel. War mages act fast in battle, using their spells to seize tactical control of a situation. Their spells strike hard, while their defensive skills foil their opponents' attempts to counterattack. War mages are also adept at turning other spellcasters' magical energy against them.

In great battles, a war mage often works with evokers, abjurers, and other types of wizards. Evokers, in particular, sometimes tease war mages for splitting their attention between offense and defense. A war mage's typical response: "What good is being able to throw a mighty fireball if I die before I can cast it?"
war.features.0.name: Arcane Deflection
war.features.1.description:
{@i 2nd-level War Magic feature}

Your keen ability to assess tactical situations allows you to act quickly in battle. You can give yourself a bonus to your initiative rolls equal to your Intelligence modifier.
war.features.1.name: Tactical Wit
war.features.2.description:
{@i 6th-level War Magic feature}

You can store magical energy within yourself to later empower your damaging spells. In its stored form, this energy is called a power surge.

You can store a maximum number of power surges equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of one). Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, your number of power surges resets to one. Whenever you successfully end a spell with {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB} or {@spell Counterspell|XPHB}, you gain one power surge, as you steal magic from the spell you foiled. If you end a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} with no power surges, you gain one power surge.

Once per turn when you deal damage to a creature or object with a wizard spell, you can spend one power surge to deal extra Force damage to that target. The extra damage equals half your Wizard level.
war.features.2.name: Power Surge
war.features.3.description:
{@i 10th-level War Magic feature}

The magic you channel helps ward off harm. While you maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB} on a spell, you have a +2 bonus to AC and all saving throws.
war.features.3.name: Durable Magic
war.features.4.description:
{@i 14th-level War Magic feature}

Your Arcane Deflection becomes infused with deadly magic. When you use your Arcane Deflection feature, you can cause magical energy to arc from you. Up to three creatures of your choice that you can see within 60 feet of you each take Force damage equal to half your Wizard level.
war.features.4.name: Deflecting Shroud
war.name: Военная Магия
war.shortDescription: Blend Evocation and Abjuration for Battle
watchers.description: The Oath of the Watchers binds Paladins to protect the mortal realm from the predations of extraplanar creatures, many of which can corrupt or destroy the unsuspecting. These Paladins are ever vigilant against the incursions of Aberrations, Celestials, Elementals, Fey, and Fiends.
watchers.features.0.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of the Watchers Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
watchers.features.0.name: Oath of the Watchers Spells
watchers.features.1.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to invest your presence with the warding power of your faith. As an action, you choose a number of creatures you can see within 30 feet of you, up to a number equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). For 1 minute, you and the chosen creatures have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws.
watchers.features.1.name: Watcher's Will
watchers.features.2.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to castigate unworldly beings. As an action, you present your {@item Holy Symbol|XPHB} and each Aberration, Celestial, Elemental, Fey, or Fiend within 30 feet of you that can hear you must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute or until it takes any damage. While {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, the creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is 0.
watchers.features.2.name: Abjure the Extraplanar
watchers.features.3.description: You emit an aura of alertness while you aren't {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}. When you and any creatures of your choice within your Aura of Protection roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, each of you gains a bonus to {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}.
watchers.features.3.name: Aura of the Sentinel
watchers.features.4.description: You've learned how to chastise anyone who dares wield beguilements against you and your wards. Whenever you or a creature you can see within 30 feet of you succeeds on an Intelligence, a Wisdom, or a Charisma saving throw, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to deal {@damage 2d8} plus your Charisma modifier in Force damage to the creature that forced the saving throw.
watchers.features.4.name: Vigilant Rebuke
watchers.features.5.description: You manifest a spark of divine power in defense of the mortal realm. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you gain the benefits below for 1 minute. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). {@b Truesight.} You gain {@variantrule Truesight|XPHB} with a range of 120 feet. {@b Advantage on Attacks.} You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against Aberrations, Celestials, Elementals, Fey, and Fiends. {@b Banishing Blow.} When you hit a creature with an attack and deal damage to it, you can force it to make a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature is magically banished to its native plane of existence if it's not currently there. On a successful save, the creature can't be banished by this feature for 24 hours.
watchers.features.5.name: Mortal Bulwark
watchers.name: Клятва Стражей
whispers.description: Most folk are happy to welcome a bard into their midst. Bards of the College of Whispers use this to their advantage. They appear to be like any other bard, sharing news, singing songs, and telling tales to the audiences they gather. In truth, the College of Whispers teaches its students that they are wolves among sheep.
whispers.features.0.description:
You gain the ability to make your weapon attacks magically toxic to a creature's mind. When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to deal an extra {@dice 2d6} psychic damage to that target. You can do so only once per round on your turn.

The psychic damage increases when you reach certain levels in this class: to {@dice 3d6} at 5th level, {@dice 5d6} at 10th level, and {@dice 8d6} at 15th level.
whispers.features.0.name: Psychic Blades
whispers.features.1.description:
You learn to infuse innocent-seeming words with an insidious magic that inspires terror. If you speak to a Humanoid alone for at least 1 minute, you can attempt to seed paranoia and fear into its mind. At the end of the conversation, the target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition of you or another creature of your choice. The target is {@condition Frightened|XPHB} in this way for 1 hour, until it is attacked or damaged, or until it witnesses its allies being attacked or damaged.

If the target succeeds on its saving throw, the target has no hint that you tried to frighten it. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
whispers.features.1.name: Words of Terror
whispers.features.2.description:
You gain the ability to adopt a Humanoid's persona. When a Humanoid dies within 30 feet of you, you can magically capture its shadow using your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}. You retain this shadow until you use it or you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

You can use the shadow as an action. When you do so, it vanishes, magically transforming into a disguise that appears on you. You now look like the dead person, but healthy and alive. This disguise lasts for 1 hour or until you end it as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}.

While you're in the disguise, you gain access to any information that the Humanoid would freely share with a casual acquaintance. Such information includes general details on its background and personal life, but doesn't include secrets. The information is enough that you can pass yourself off as the person by drawing on its memories.

Another creature can see through this disguise by succeeding on a Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check contested by your Charisma ({@skill Deception}) check. You gain a +5 bonus to your check.

Once you capture a shadow with this feature, you can't capture another one with it until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
whispers.features.2.name: Mantle of Whispers
whispers.features.3.description:
You gain the ability to weave dark magic into your words and tap into a creature's deepest fears. As an action, you magically whisper a phrase that only one creature of your choice within 30 feet of you can hear. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. It automatically succeeds if it doesn't share a language with you or if it can't hear you. On a successful saving throw, your whisper sounds like unintelligible mumbling and has no effect.

If the target fails its saving throw, it has the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition for the next 8 hours or until you or your allies attack or damage it. It interprets the whispers as a description of its most mortifying secret.

While you gain no knowledge of this secret, the target is convinced you know it. While {@condition Charmed|XPHB} in this way, the creature obeys your commands for fear that you will reveal its secret. It won't risk its life for you or fight for you, unless it was already inclined to do so. It grants you favors and gifts it would offer to a close friend.

When the effect ends, the creature has no understanding of why it held you in such fear.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
whispers.features.3.name: Shadow Lore
whispers.name: Коллегия Шёпотов
whispers.shortDescription: Use Secrets and Fears as Weapons
white-hat.description: Некоторые Стрелки живут по кодексу и ожидают того же от других. Эти Стрелки, известные как Белые Шляпы, иногда служат блюстителями закона, но никогда не колеблются делать то, что правильно, когда закон говорит обратное. Несмотря на любовь к смертоносному оружию, Белые Шляпы предпочитают сохранять друзей в безопасности и подавлять врагов ненасильственно — предпочтение, на которое их враги соглашаются нечасто.
white-hat.features.0.description: [Манёвр] Вы можете {@action Bonus Action|XPHB|Бонусным действием} потратить одну Кость риска, чтобы следить за опасностями, угрожающими вашим спутникам. Выберите союзника, видимого вами в пределах 60 футов. Этот союзник получает {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB|Временные хиты} в количестве, равном выпавшему на Кости риска числу. До начала вашего следующего хода, если по союзнику попадает атака, вы можете {@action Reaction|XPHB|Реакцией} совершить дальнобойную атаку оружием по атакующему.
white-hat.features.0.name: Установить закон
white-hat.features.1.description: Аура стоического спокойствия исходит от вас в виде {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB|Эманации} с радиусом 10 футов. Вы и союзники в {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB|Эманации} совершаете с {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB|Преимуществом} спасброски, чтобы избежать или прекратить состояние {@condition Frightened|XPHB|Испуганный}. Аура неактивна, пока у вас состояние {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB|Недееспособный}.
white-hat.features.1.name: Аура стального взгляда
white-hat.features.2.description: Когда вы наносите {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB|Критическое попадание} существу, вы призываете цель сдаться вместо того, чтобы засадить в неё снаряд. Цель должна совершить успешный спасбросок Мудрости против Сл спасброска от вашего Манёвра или получить состояния {@condition Frightened|XPHB|Испуганный} и {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB|Недееспособный} на 1 минуту. Эти состояния прекращаются раньше, если существо получает любой урон, если у вас состояние {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB|Недееспособный} или если вы умираете. Существо может повторять спасбросок Мудрости в конце каждого своего хода, прекращая состояния на себе при успехе.
white-hat.features.2.name: Руки вверх
white-hat.features.3.description: Один раз за ход, когда вы попадаете по существу с размером Большое или меньше дальнобойной атакой оружием, вы можете охромить цель. Существо не может перемещаться в свой следующий ход, если только сначала не совершит действие {@action Disengage|XPHB|Отход}.
white-hat.features.3.name: Длинная рука закона
white-hat.features.4.description: Через стрелковый героизм вы получаете следующие преимущества.
white-hat.features.4.details.improvedAur: Радиус вашего умения Аура стального взгляда увеличивается до 30 футов.
white-hat.features.4.details.ironCladLaw: Когда вы используете манёвр Установить закон, союзник обладает {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Сопротивлением} к Дробящему, Колющему и Рубящему урону до начала вашего следующего хода.
white-hat.features.4.details.stunnedSurr: Когда существо проваливает спасбросок против вашего умения Руки вверх, оно вместо состояния {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB|Недееспособный} получает состояние {@condition Stunned|XPHB|Ошеломлённый}.
white-hat.features.4.name: Герой золотой звезды
white-hat.name: Белая Шляпа
wild-heart.description: Barbarians who follow the Path of the Wild Heart view themselves as kin to animals. These Barbarians learn magical means to communicate with animals, and their Rage heightens their connection to animals as it fills them with supernatural might.
wild-heart.features.0.description: You can cast the {@spell Beast Sense|XPHB} and {@spell Speak with Animals|XPHB} spells but only as Rituals. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for them.
wild-heart.features.0.name: Animal Speaker
wild-heart.features.1.description: Your Rage taps into the primal power of animals. Whenever you activate your Rage, you gain one of the following options of your choice.
wild-heart.features.1.details.bear: While your Rage is active, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to every damage type except Force, Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant.
wild-heart.features.1.details.eagle: When you activate your Rage, you can take the {@action Disengage|XPHB} and {@action Dash|XPHB} actions as part of that {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}. While your Rage is active, you can take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to take both of those actions.
wild-heart.features.1.details.wolf: While your Rage is active, your allies have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against any enemy of yours within 5 feet of you.
wild-heart.features.1.name: Rage of the Wilds
wild-heart.features.2.description: You gain one of the following options of your choice. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change your choice.
wild-heart.features.2.details.owl: You have {@variantrule Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet. If you already have {@variantrule Darkvision|XPHB}, its range increases by 60 feet.
wild-heart.features.2.details.panther: You have a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
wild-heart.features.2.details.salmon: You have a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
wild-heart.features.2.name: Aspect of the Wilds
wild-heart.features.3.description: You can cast the {@spell Commune with Nature|XPHB} spell but only as a Ritual. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.
wild-heart.features.3.name: Nature Speaker
wild-heart.features.4.description: Whenever you activate your Rage, you gain one of the following options of your choice.
wild-heart.features.4.details.falcon: While your Rage is active, you have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} if you aren't wearing any armor.
wild-heart.features.4.details.lion: While your Rage is active, any of your enemies within 5 feet of you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against targets other than you or another Barbarian who has this option active.
wild-heart.features.4.details.ram: While your Rage is active, you can cause a Large or smaller creature to have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition when you hit it with a melee attack.
wild-heart.features.4.name: Power of the Wilds
wild-heart.name: Путь Дикого Сердца
wild-heart.shortDescription: Walk in Community with the Animal World
wild-magic.description: Your innate magic stems from the forces of chaos that underlie the order of creation. You or an ancestor might have endured exposure to raw magic, perhaps through a planar portal leading to Limbo or the Elemental Planes. Perhaps you were blessed by a fey being or marked by a demon. Or your magic could be a fluke with no apparent cause. Whatever its source, this magic churns within you, waiting for any outlet.
wild-magic.features.0.description: Your spellcasting can unleash surges of untamed magic. Once per turn, you can roll {@dice 1d20} immediately after you cast a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot. If you roll a 20, roll on the Wild Magic Surge table to create a magical effect. If the magical effect is a spell, it is too wild to be affected by your Metamagic.
wild-magic.features.0.name: Wild Magic Surge
wild-magic.features.1.description: You can manipulate the forces of chance and chaos to give yourself {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on one {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} before you roll the {@dice d20}. Once you do so, you must cast a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot or finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can use this feature again. If you do cast a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot before you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you automatically roll on the Wild Magic Surge table.
wild-magic.features.1.name: Tides of Chaos
wild-magic.features.2.description: You have the ability to twist fate using your wild magic. Immediately after another creature you can see rolls the {@dice d20} for a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and spend 1 Sorcery Point to roll {@dice 1d4} and apply the number rolled as a bonus or penalty (your choice) to the {@dice d20} roll.
wild-magic.features.2.details.attackBonu: For 10 minutes, the creature can roll a {@dice d3} whenever making an attack roll or an ability check and add the number rolled to the {@dice d20} roll.
wild-magic.features.2.details.spellSlot: Roll a {@dice d3}. The creature regains one expended spell slot, the level of which equals the number rolled or lower (the creature's choice). Once a creature receives this benefit, that creature can't receive it again until after a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
wild-magic.features.2.name: Bend Luck
wild-magic.features.3.description: You gain a modicum of control over the surges of your wild magic. Whenever you roll on the Wild Magic Surge table, you can roll twice and use either number.
wild-magic.features.3.name: Controlled Chaos
wild-magic.features.4.description:
Immediately after you cast a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot, you can create an effect of your choice from the Wild Magic Surge table instead of rolling on that table. You can choose any effect in the table except for the final row, and if the chosen effect involves a roll, you must make it.

Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
wild-magic.features.4.name: Tamed Surge
wild-magic.name: Дикая Магия
wildfire.description: This subclass is from a different game edition. For a given subclass feature, you may gain that feature at a different level from the one specified in the subclass feature. Druids within the Circle of Wildfire understand that destruction is sometimes the precursor of creation, such as when a forest fire promotes later growth. These druids bond with a primal spirit that harbors both destructive and creative power, allowing the druids to create controlled flames that burn away one thing but give life to another.
wildfire.features.0.description: You have formed a bond with a wildfire spirit, a primal being of creation and destruction. Your link with this spirit grants you access to some spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown on the Circle of Wildfire Spells table. Once you gain access to one of these spells, you always have it prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day. If you gain access to a spell that doesn't appear on the druid spell list, the spell is nonetheless a druid spell for you.
wildfire.features.0.name: Circle Spells
wildfire.features.1.description: You can summon the primal spirit bound to your soul. As an action, you can expend one use of your Wild Shape feature to summon your wildfire spirit, rather than assuming a beast form. The spirit appears in an unoccupied space of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of you. Each creature within 10 feet of the spirit (other than you) when it appears must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC or take {@dice 2d6} fire damage. The spirit is friendly to you and your companions and obeys your commands. See this creature's game statistics in the {@creature Wildfire Spirit} stat block, which uses your proficiency bonus (PB) in several places. You determine the spirit's appearance. Some spirits take the form of a humanoid figure made of gnarled branches covered in flame, while others look like beasts wreathed in fire. In combat, the spirit shares your initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. The only action it takes on its turn is the {@action Dodge} action, unless you take a bonus action on your turn to command it to take another action. That action can be one in its stat block or some other action. If you are {@condition Incapacitated}, the spirit can take any action of its choice, not just {@action Dodge}. The spirit manifests for 1 hour, until it is reduced to 0 hit points, until you use this feature to summon the spirit again, or until you die.
wildfire.features.1.name: Summon Wildfire Spirit
wildfire.features.2.description: The bond with your wildfire spirit enhances your destructive and restorative spells. Whenever you cast a spell that deals fire damage or restores hit points while your wildfire spirit is summoned, roll a {@dice d8}, and you gain a bonus equal to the number rolled to one damage or healing roll of the spell. In addition, when you cast a spell with a range other than self, the spell can originate from you or from your wildfire spirit.
wildfire.features.2.name: Enhanced Bond
wildfire.features.3.description: You gain the ability to turn death into magical flames that can heal or incinerate. When a Small or larger creature dies within 30 feet of you or your wildfire spirit, a harmless spectral flame springs forth in the dead creature's space and flickers there for 1 minute. When a creature you can see enters that space, you can use your reaction to extinguish the spectral flame there and either heal the creature or deal fire damage to it. The healing or damage equals {@dice 2d10} + your Wisdom modifier. You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
wildfire.features.3.name: Cauterizing Flames
wildfire.features.4.description: The bond with your wildfire spirit can save you from death. If the spirit is within 120 feet of you when you are reduced to 0 hit points and thereby fall {@condition Unconscious}, you can cause the spirit to drop to 0 hit points. You then regain half your hit points and immediately rise to your feet. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.
wildfire.features.4.name: Blazing Revival
wildfire.name: Круг Лесного Пожара
winter-walker.description: Winter Walkers hone their craft in the bleak and frozen wilds of places like Icewind Dale. These ruthless, rimed Rangers hunt monsters that haunt arctic wastelands, eventually becoming frigid terrors themselves. Winter Walkers are well versed in the phenomena of Icewind Dale, including the latent magic of fallen Netherese cities, endemic monsters like yetis and crag cats, and the rising threat of Underdark invaders. Due to their cold pragmatism, terrifying magic, and mastery of the region, Winter Walkers are regarded with equal parts respect and fear.
winter-walker.features.0.details.bitingC: {@b Biting Cold.} Damage from your weapon attacks, Ranger spells, and Ranger features ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage.
winter-walker.features.0.details.frostRe: {@b Frost Resistance.} You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage.
winter-walker.features.0.details.polarSt: {@b Polar Strikes.} When you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon, you can deal an extra {@damage 1d4} Cold damage to the target, which can take this extra damage only once per turn. When you reach Ranger level 11, this extra damage increases to {@damage 1d6}.
winter-walker.features.0.name: Frigid Explorer
winter-walker.features.1.description: Ice rimes you and your prey, protecting you and slowing them. When you cast {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB}, you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@damage 1d10} plus your Ranger level. Additionally, while a creature is marked by your {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB}, it can't take the {@action Disengage|XPHB} action.
winter-walker.features.1.name: Hunter's Rime
winter-walker.features.2.description: When you reach a Ranger level specified in the Winter Walker Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
winter-walker.features.2.name: Winter Walker Spells
winter-walker.features.3.description: Your experience surviving harrowing environments allows you to bolster your allies in addition to yourself. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, choose a number of creatures you can see equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of one). Each chosen creature regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@damage 1d10} plus your Ranger level and has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 hour. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
winter-walker.features.3.name: Fortifying Soul
winter-walker.features.4.description: When a creature hits you with an attack roll, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to force the creature to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn. While the target is {@condition Stunned|XPHB}, its Speed is reduced to 0 feet. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
winter-walker.features.4.name: Chilling Retribution
winter-walker.features.5.description: When you cast {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB}, you can adopt a ghostly, snowy form. This form lasts until the spell ends, and while you are in this form, you gain the following benefits. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you expend a level 4+ spell slot (no action required).
winter-walker.features.5.details.frozenS: {@b Frozen Soul.} You have Immunity to Cold damage. When you first adopt this form and at the start of each of your subsequent turns, each creature of your choice in a 15-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} originating from you takes {@damage 2d4} Cold damage.
winter-walker.features.5.details.partial: {@b Partially Incorporeal.} You have Immunity to the {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, {@condition Prone|XPHB}, and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions. You can move through creatures and objects as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}, but you take {@damage 1d10} Force damage if you end your turn inside a creature or an object. If the form ends while you are inside a creature or an object, you are shunted to the nearest unoccupied space.
winter-walker.features.5.name: Frozen Haunt
winter-walker.name: Зимний Скиталец
winter-walker.shortDescription: Withstand the Horrors of Frigid Wastelands
world-tree.description: Barbarians who follow the Path of the World Tree connect with the cosmic tree Yggdrasil through their Rage. This tree grows among the Outer Planes, connecting them to each other and the Material Plane. These Barbarians draw on the tree's magic for vitality and as a means of dimensional travel.
world-tree.features.0.description: Your Rage taps into the life force of the World Tree. You gain the following benefits.
world-tree.features.0.details.lifeGiving: At the start of each of your turns while your Rage is active, you can choose another creature within 10 feet of yourself to gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}. To determine the number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}, roll a number of {@dice d6}s equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and add them together. If any of those {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} remain when your Rage ends, they vanish.
world-tree.features.0.details.vitalitySu: When you activate your Rage, you gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Barbarian level.
world-tree.features.0.name: Vitality of the Tree
world-tree.features.1.description: Whenever a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 feet of you while your Rage is active, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to summon spectral branches of the World Tree around it. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}) or be teleported to an unoccupied space you can see within 5 feet of yourself or in the nearest unoccupied space you can see. After the target teleports, you can reduce its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} to 0 until the end of the current turn.
world-tree.features.1.name: Branches of the Tree
world-tree.features.2.description: During your turn, your reach is 10 feet greater with any {@variantrule Melee|XPHB} weapon that has the {@variantrule Heavy|XPHB} or {@variantrule Versatile|XPHB} property, as tendrils of the World Tree extend from you. When you hit with such a weapon on your turn, you can activate the {@variantrule Push|XPHB} or {@variantrule Topple|XPHB} mastery property in addition to a different mastery property you're using with that weapon.
world-tree.features.2.name: Battering Roots
world-tree.features.3.description: You activate your Rage and as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} while your Rage is active, you can teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. In addition, once per Rage, you can also bring up to six willing creatures who are within 10 feet of you. Each creature teleports to an unoccupied space of your choice within 10 feet of your destination space.
world-tree.features.3.name: Travel Along the Tree
world-tree.name: Путь Мирового Древа
world-tree.shortDescription: Trace the Roots and Branches of the Multiverse
wrathful-dead.description: Barbarians who walk the Path of the Wrathful Dead commune with the spirits of the deceased and channel their spectral powers. These Barbarians are often preoccupied with the dead whispering in their ears, which urge them to act upon the whims and emotions of the haunting spirits. Some who walk this path learn to master the powers gifted to them; others find they've become servants to the unending chorus clamoring for their attention.
wrathful-dead.features.0.description: Your Rage taps into the endless fury of the unquiet dead. While your Rage is active, you take on aspects of restless spirits and you gain the following benefits.
wrathful-dead.features.0.details.shadowF: You ignore {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. In addition, you can move through the space of any creature, but you can't end your move in an occupied space.
wrathful-dead.features.0.details.shadowy: Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet, and {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} against you.
wrathful-dead.features.0.details.spectra: You see creatures and objects within 120 feet that have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition as if they were visible, and you can see into the Ethereal Plane.
wrathful-dead.features.0.name: Rage of the Dead
wrathful-dead.features.1.description: You are overcome by an emotion a nearby spirit experienced at its death. You gain one of the following options of your choice. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change your choice.
wrathful-dead.features.1.details.hate: While your Rage is active, your attacks with weapons and {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}.
wrathful-dead.features.1.details.jealous: Whenever a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 feet of you while your Rage is active, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to summon spectral assailants to {@condition Grapple|XPHB} the creature. The creature makes a Strength or Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Constitution modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}). On a failure, the creature has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition until the end of its turn. On a successful save, the creature is not {@condition Grappled|XPHB}; however, each foot of movement costs 1 extra foot for that creature until the end of its turn.
wrathful-dead.features.1.details.terror: Whenever a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 feet of you while your Rage is active, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make that creature terrified until the start of its next turn. A terrified creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved and {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} against it have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.
wrathful-dead.features.1.name: Final Night Catharsis
wrathful-dead.features.2.description: Once per active Rage, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to channel a traumatic death. When you do, choose one of the following options.
wrathful-dead.features.2.details.contami: At the start of their turn, each creature of your choice in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} originating from you must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus your Constitution modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}) or take Poison damage and gain the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. To determine the Poison damage, roll a number of {@dice d6}s equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and add them together. An affected creature can use an action to end the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition on itself.
wrathful-dead.features.2.details.hypothe: At the start of their turn, each creature of your choice in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} originating from you must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}) or take Cold damage and have its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} halved for 1 minute. To determine the Cold damage, roll a number of {@dice d6}s equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and add them together. A creature can use an action to end this effect on itself.
wrathful-dead.features.2.details.immolat: At the start of their turn, each creature of your choice in a 15-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} originating from you must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Constitution modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}) or take Fire damage. To determine the Fire damage, roll a number of {@dice d6}s equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and add them together. A creature can extinguish the fire on itself by giving itself the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition and rolling on the ground. The fire also goes out if it is doused, submerged, or suffocated.
wrathful-dead.features.2.name: Dark Doom Revisited
wrathful-dead.features.3.description: Your familiarity with death has left you resistant to its call. You gain the following benefits.
wrathful-dead.features.3.details.hardToK: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throws|XPHB}. In addition, you must fail four {@variantrule Death Saving Throws|XPHB} to die instead of three as normal.
wrathful-dead.features.3.details.returnT: You can call the spirits of the deceased to restore life essence to a nearby creature. You can cast the {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB}, {@spell Raise Dead|XPHB}, or {@spell Revivify|XPHB} spell without providing Material components. When you do, you gain 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level for {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB}, 2 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} levels for {@spell Revivify|XPHB}, and 3 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} levels for {@spell Raise Dead|XPHB}. Constitution is your spellcasting ability for this feature. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you expend two uses of your Rage (no action required) to restore your use of it.
wrathful-dead.features.3.name: Death Is but a Door
wrathful-dead.features.4.description: Your Rage is empowered by the overwhelming emotions of the unquiet dead. Your Final Night Catharsis feature grants an additional effect based on the chosen emotion.
wrathful-dead.features.4.details.hate: While your Rage is active, your attacks with weapons and {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}.
wrathful-dead.features.4.details.jealous: Whenever a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 feet of you while your Rage is active, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to summon spectral assailants to {@condition Grapple|XPHB} the creature. The creature makes a Strength or Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Constitution modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}). On a failure, the creature has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition until the end of its turn. On a successful save, the creature is not {@condition Grappled|XPHB}; however, each foot of movement costs 1 extra foot for that creature until the end of its turn.
wrathful-dead.features.4.details.terror: Whenever a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 feet of you while your Rage is active, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make that creature terrified until the start of its next turn. A terrified creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved and {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} against it have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.
wrathful-dead.features.4.name: Powered by Pathos
wrathful-dead.name: Путь Гневных Мертвецов
wrathful-dead.shortDescription: Channel the Rage of the Unquiet Dead
wretched.description: With every promise whispered to a fairy, every contract signed with a devil, and every pact made with the dead, there's a chance someone doesn't keep their end of the bargain. The vengeance of immortal beings lasts much longer than one lifetime, and the lingering magic of these curses can affect the mortal's descendants. These inherited magical afflictions may manifest as a plague, deformity, or aversion to the sun. In such families, children may be born who learn to master the latent magic within their inherited curses, turning their banes into boons—these sorcerers are known collectively as the Wretched.
wretched.features.0.description: You have the ability to cast a sliver of your curse onto another temporarily. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose a creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. The chosen creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the next {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} it makes before the start of your next turn. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you spend 1 Sorcery Point (no action required) to restore your use of it.
wretched.features.0.name: Bad Luck Charm
wretched.features.1.description: Your senses easily attune to the supernatural forces that caused your inherited affliction. You always have the {@spell Detect Evil and Good|XPHB} spell prepared and can cast it without expending a spell slot. In addition, choose one of the following types of creatures as the being that cursed your ancestor: Fey, Fiend, or Undead. On each of your turns while you maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB} on {@spell Detect Evil and Good|XPHB}, including the turn when you cast it, creatures of the chosen type have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against you, and you can't be possessed, {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} by such creatures. Once you cast the spell with this feature, you can't do so in this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
wretched.features.1.name: Blood Ties
wretched.features.2.description: You suffer from a curse inherited from an ancestor who failed to uphold their end of a bargain with an otherworldly power. Choose one of the following curses that was passed down to you.
wretched.features.2.details.hulking: {@b Hulking.} Your ancestor was cursed with a hulking frame. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks to escape notice by moving quietly. In addition, your {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB} maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 whenever you gain another Sorcerer level. Finally, you count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity.
wretched.features.2.details.nocturnal: {@b Nocturnal.} Your ancestor was cursed to shun the light of day. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight while you are in sunlight. In addition, you can see normally in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} and {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}—both magical and nonmagical—within 120 feet of yourself.
wretched.features.2.details.plaguebearer: {@b Plaguebearer.} Your ancestor was cursed with physical symptoms of a plague. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) checks made to influence an Indifferent Humanoid within 5 feet of yourself. In addition, you are immune to the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition and have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage.
wretched.features.2.name: Wretched Curse
wretched.features.3.description: You always have the {@spell Bestow Curse|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast the spell by spending 3 Sorcery Points instead of a spell slot. When you cast the spell in this way, the spell doesn't require {@status Concentration|XPHB}, and its range changes to 60 feet for that casting.
wretched.features.3.name: Share the Burden
wretched.features.4.description:
As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can adopt the terrifying visage of the being that cursed your ancestor for 10 minutes. During this time, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to cause creatures of your choice you can see within 30 feet of yourself to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn. In addition, while your Terrifying Visage feature is active, you gain the following benefit based on the creature type chosen with your Blood Ties feature.

{@b Fey.} As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

{@b Fiend.} You have Resistance to Cold and Fire damage.

{@b Undead.} When you take damage of any type other than Radiant, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to reduce the damage by half your Sorcerer level.

Once you use Terrifying Visage, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
wretched.features.4.name: Terrifying Visage
wretched.features.5.description:
Your magic has become powerful enough that you can call and command a servant of those who cursed you. Choose one of the following creatures based on the choice you made with your Blood Ties feature: {@creature Lamia} or {@creature Troll} (Fey only), {@creature Barbed Devil} or {@creature Incubus}/{@creature Succubus} (Fiend only), {@creature Ghost} or {@creature Wraith} (Undead only).

You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and spend 5 Sorcery Points to summon your chosen creature. The creature appears in an unoccupied space you can see within 60 feet. It disappears when it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you use this feature to summon another creature, or after 10 minutes have passed.

The creature is an ally to you and your allies. In combat, the creature shares your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action and uses its movement to avoid danger.
wretched.features.5.name: Vengeful Summons
wretched.name: Отверженный
wretched.shortDescription: Wield Your Curse Like a Weapon
zeal.description: Zealots who take the Oath of Zeal are devoted to rooting out heresy and corruption wherever they find it. They serve as inquisitors, judges, and executioners, wielding divine authority to expose lies and punish the wicked. Their fervent devotion grants them supernatural insight and the power to compel truth from even the most stubborn deceivers.
zeal.features.0.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of Zeal Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
zeal.features.0.name: Oath of Zeal Spells
zeal.features.1.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to mark a creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself as a heretic. For 1 minute, your weapon attacks and {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} against the chosen creature can score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}. In addition, whenever the target starts its turn, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make a melee attack against that creature if it's within reach.
zeal.features.1.name: Mark of the Heretic
zeal.features.2.description: You and your allies have Immunity to the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition while in your Aura of Protection. If a {@condition Blinded|XPHB} ally enters the aura, that condition has no effect on that ally while there. Additionally, you can see {@condition Invisible|XPHB} creatures within your Aura of Protection.
zeal.features.2.name: Aura of Clarity
zeal.features.3.description: You can cast {@spell Zone of Truth|XPHB} without expending a spell slot. In addition, a creature that succeeds on its saving throw takes {@damage 1d6} Psychic damage at the start of each of its turns while in your {@spell Zone of Truth|XPHB} until it chooses to fail its saving throw instead.
zeal.features.3.name: Compel Confession
zeal.features.4.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can reveal the true nature of your enemies for 1 minute. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). You gain the following benefits. {@b Blinding Glory.} Enemies that start their turn within 5 feet of you must make a Constitution saving throw against the Paladin's spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition until the start of its next turn. {@b See the Truth.} You have {@variantrule Truesight|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet. {@b Smite the Heretic.} As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can choose a creature within 60 feet of yourself and reveal its weaknesses. You and your allies have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against that creature.
zeal.features.4.name: Apocalyptic Revelation
zeal.name: Клятва Рвения
zealot.description: Barbarians who walk the Path of the Zealot receive boons from a god or pantheon. These Barbarians experience their Rage as an ecstatic episode of divine union that infuses them with power. They are often allies to the priests and other followers of their god or pantheon.
zealot.features.0.description: You can channel divine power into your strikes. On each of your turns while your Rage is active, the first creature you hit with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} takes extra damage equal to {@dice 1d6} plus half your Barbarian level (round down). The extra damage is Necrotic or Radiant; you choose the type each time you deal the damage.
zealot.features.0.name: Divine Fury
zealot.features.1.description: A divine entity helps ensure you can continue the fight. You have a pool of four {@dice d12}s that you can spend to heal yourself. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend dice from the pool, roll them, and regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the roll's total. Your pool regains all expended dice when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. The pool's maximum number of dice increases by one when you reach Barbarian levels 6 (5 dice), 12 (6 dice), and 17 (7 dice).
zealot.features.1.name: Warrior of the Gods
zealot.features.2.description: Once per active Rage, if you fail a saving throw, you can reroll it with a bonus equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and you must use the new roll.
zealot.features.2.name: Fanatical Focus
zealot.features.3.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you unleash a battle cry infused with divine energy. Up to ten other creatures of your choice within 60 feet of you gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls and saving throws until the start of your next turn. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you expend a use of your Rage (no action required) to restore your use of it.
zealot.features.3.name: Zealous Presence
zealot.features.4.description: When you activate your Rage, you can assume the form of a divine warrior. This form lasts for 1 minute or until you drop to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. While in this form, you gain the benefits below.
zealot.features.4.details.flight: You have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and can hover.
zealot.features.4.details.resistance: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant damage.
zealot.features.4.details.revivification: When a creature within 30 feet of you would drop to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to expend a use of your Rage to instead change the target's {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to a number equal to your Barbarian level.
zealot.features.4.name: Rage of the Gods
zealot.name: Путь Фанатика
zealot.shortDescription: Rage in Ecstatic Union with a God
